fields.notes:
Description: Parexel is a prominent global Clinical Research Organization (CRO) and biopharmaceutical outsourcing partner, dedicated to providing insights-driven clinical and consulting solutions for the life sciences industry. Founded in 1982 and headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, Parexel operates in over 100 countries and employs approximately 21,000 to 24,000 professionals worldwide. The company is owned by EQT Private Equity and Goldman Sachs Asset Management and collaborates with technology firms like Microsoft and Synairgen.

Parexel offers a comprehensive range of services throughout drug development, including clinical trial management, data and biostatistics, regulatory consulting, medical affairs, and real-world evidence studies. The company also provides specialized solutions such as decentralized clinical trials and risk-based quality management. Its target customers include pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and medical device industries, serving both established companies and emerging innovators. By focusing on patient-centered clinical trials, Parexel aims to enhance access to clinical research globally.

fields.name: Pacific Biosciences
fields.address: 1305 O'Brien Drive, Menlo Park, California, United States, 94025
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 718660527; Latest funding: Other (84760527) on 2023-10-01
fields.notes:
Description: At PacBio, we're devoted to empowering you. Pioneering the future through biology takes vision. You have to look beyond the status quo to what's possible.

We know your vision can reveal the full potential of the genome, advance human health and the health of our planet. And that is why your vision and pursuit are at the very center of what our company does — our product design, our customer support model, our partnerships, and even our identity.

We create the world's most advanced sequencing systems to provide you the most complete and accurate view of genomes, transcriptomes, and epigenomes. Our founders invented a new way to study the synthesis and regulation of DNA, RNA, and proteins. Driven by a desire to advance human health, we're harnessing advances in biochemistry, optics, nanofabrication, and more.

We are passionate about developing products that empower scientists to explore the full spectrum of genetic variation in any organism — from unraveling the mystery of rare diseases to improving the world's food supply. With world-class service and support, we keep your research progressing so you can focus on finding answers.

We encourage you to see the whole picture — and consider us the partner you trust to see it too.

fields.name: Abzena
fields.address: 6325 Lusk Blvd, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 124423185; Latest funding: Private Equity (65000000) on 2022-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Abzena is a leading Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) and Contract Research Organization (CRO) that specializes in the development of complex biologics and bioconjugates. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in San Diego, California, Abzena operates research, development, and cGMP manufacturing facilities in San Diego, Bristol, and Cambridge. The company focuses on accelerating the development of life-changing medicines from discovery through commercial launch.

Abzena offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire drug lifecycle, including antibody discovery, protein engineering, ADC linker development, and GMP manufacturing. They provide tailored strategies for biopharmaceutical programs, supporting clients with fully integrated programs or individual services. Their expertise extends to developing novel bioconjugates and antibody-drug conjugates, as well as specialized linker technologies and immunogenicity assessment tools. Abzena partners with biotech and biopharma companies, academic groups, and clients in the biopharmaceutical industry to streamline the development of new treatments.

raw_data.Short Description:
Abzena is a leading Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) and Contract Research Organization (CRO) that specializes in the development of complex biologics and bioconjugates. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in San Diego, California, Abzena operates research, development, and cGMP manufacturing facilities in San Diego, Bristol, and Cambridge. The company focuses on accelerating the development of life-changing medicines from discovery through commercial launch.

Abzena offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire drug lifecycle, including antibody discovery, protein engineering, ADC linker development, and GMP manufacturing. They provide tailored strategies for biopharmaceutical programs, supporting clients with fully integrated programs or individual services. Their expertise extends to developing novel bioconjugates and antibody-drug conjugates, as well as specialized linker technologies and immunogenicity assessment tools. Abzena partners with biotech and biopharma companies, academic groups, and clients in the biopharmaceutical industry to streamline the development of new treatments.

fields.name: BioGenex
fields.address: 48810 Kato Rd, Fremont, California, United States, 94538-7309
fields.notes:
Description: BioGenex is a U.S.-based molecular pathology company founded in 1981, specializing in automated systems and reagents for tumor diagnosis, prognosis, and precision medicine. The company is committed to enhancing the capabilities of pathologists and clinicians to improve cancer patient outcomes. With headquarters in Fremont, California, and a regional center in Hyderabad, India, BioGenex operates globally, serving laboratories through direct sales and partnerships.

The company offers a comprehensive solution for slide-based cell and tissue analysis, featuring fully automated molecular pathology workstations and advanced workflow solutions like the eFISHency integrated workflow. BioGenex's product portfolio includes automated systems, IHC/ISH/FISH reagents, and detection kits, all designed to support high-throughput and sensitive tumor analysis. With a focus on innovation and customer support, BioGenex positions itself as a leader in the molecular diagnostics field, catering to pathology laboratories, hospitals, and life science research institutions worldwide.

fields.name: Hzymes Inc

fields.notes:
Description: Hzymes is a leading high-tech enterprise dedicated to the research and development, manufacturing, and sales of in vitro diagnostic (IVD) products and Biopharma Products. We specialize in providing exceptional quality enzymes, coenzymes, enzyme substrates, and biochemistry IVD reagents. With our cutting-edge solutions, we strive to revolutionize the field of diagnostics and contribute to the well-being of individuals worldwide.

At Hzymes, we pride ourselves on our exceptional R&D team, comprising highly skilled professionals with decades of experience in enzyme engineering. Their expertise and dedication drive our success, enabling us to deliver top-performance enzyme molecules and reagents with unrivaled speed and quality. This team of experts is our most valuable asset and a constant source of innovation, propelling us to the forefront of the industry.

Based in China, Hzymes operates in a country with a population of 1.3 billion people. We recognize the responsibility and privilege that comes with serving such a vast community. Our mission is to ensure that more individuals can enjoy a happy and healthy life. This pursuit fuels our determination to make a difference, motivating us to push boundaries and overcome challenges in the field of diagnostics.

At Hzymes, we are driven by our unwavering commitment to delivering accurate and precise results through our enzyme molecules and reagents. We believe that by providing innovative IVD solutions, we can empower healthcare professionals and individuals to make informed decisions about their health. Our products are designed to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of diagnostic processes, enabling better patient outcomes.

We understand the global impact of our work and embrace the opportunity to contribute to the improvement of lives worldwide. raw_data.Short Description:
Hzymes is a leading high-tech enterprise dedicated to the research and development, manufacturing, and sales of in vitro diagnostic (IVD) products and Biopharma Products. We specialize in providing exceptional quality enzymes, coenzymes, enzyme substrates, and biochemistry IVD reagents. With our cutting-edge solutions, we strive to revolutionize the field of diagnostics and contribute to the well-being of individuals worldwide.

At Hzymes, we pride ourselves on our exceptional R&D team, comprising highly skilled professionals with decades of experience in enzyme engineering. Their expertise and dedication drive our success, enabling us to deliver top-performance enzyme molecules and reagents with unrivaled speed and quality. This team of experts is our most valuable asset and a constant source of innovation, propelling us to the forefront of the industry.

Based in China, Hzymes operates in a country with a population of 1.3 billion people. We recognize the responsibility and privilege that comes with serving such a vast community. Our mission is to ensure that more individuals can enjoy a happy and healthy life. This pursuit fuels our determination to make a difference, motivating us to push boundaries and overcome challenges in the field of diagnostics.

At Hzymes, we are driven by our unwavering commitment to delivering accurate and precise results through our enzyme molecules and reagents. We believe that by providing innovative IVD solutions, we can empower healthcare professionals and individuals to make informed decisions about their health. Our products are designed to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of diagnostic processes, enabling better patient outcomes.

We understand the global impact of our work and embrace the opportunity to contribute to the improvement of lives worldwide. Description: Accutek Packaging Equipment Companies, Inc. is a leading privately held packaging machinery manufacturer in the United States, specializing in turnkey packaging solutions. With a focus on customer support, product innovation, and competitive pricing, we strive to be the preferred choice for packaging equipment, providing comprehensive solutions to drive success in the industry.

fields.name: SaniSure
fields.address: 1020 Flynn Rd, Camarillo, California, United States, 93012
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2020-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: SaniSure is a global leader in single-use technologies (SUT) for bioprocessing, focusing on complex flow paths and closed system designs. Founded in 1992, the company operates from its headquarters in Camarillo, California, and has five manufacturing sites worldwide, ensuring supply-chain security. SaniSure aims to simplify biopharmaceutical manufacturing through innovation and vertical integration.

The company offers a range of products, including single-use items like bottles, bags, and tubing, as well as innovative solutions such as mixing systems and integrated platforms for end-to-end workflows. SaniSure also provides engineering, design, and R&D services tailored to aseptic solutions, along with custom assemblies and prototyping for various bioprocessing steps. Its target customers include the biopharmaceutical industry, particularly those involved in drug and vaccine manufacturing, cell culture, and advanced therapies.

fields.name: STEMCELL Technologies
fields.address: 1618 Station Street, Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada, V6A 1B6
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 64100000; Latest funding: Other (36590000) on 2025-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: STEMCELL Technologies is a Canadian biotechnology company based in Vancouver, founded in 1993. It is the world's largest dedicated supplier of specialized cell culture media, cell separation technologies, instruments, and accessory products for life science research. The company was established by Dr. Allen Eaves to provide standardized, cost-effective cell culture media for hematopoietic stem cells and has since expanded into a global enterprise with offices in multiple countries.

The company offers a wide range of over 2,500 specialized research tools and services aimed at advancing scientific research. Their core products include high-quality cell culture media, immunomagnetic reagents for cell separation, and automated cell separation instruments. Notable product lines feature STEMdiff organoid differentiation kits, EasySep immunomagnetic cell isolation kits, and RoboSep systems. STEMCELL also provides primary cells, cytokines, antibodies, and educational resources such as training courses and technical support. Their customer base includes academic laboratories, biotechnology companies, and pharmaceutical researchers across more than 120 countries.

fields.name: A&M Biomedical
fields.address: 23042 Alcalde Drive, Laguna Hills, California, United States, 92653
fields.notes:
Description: A&M Biomedical Inc. is a woman-owned contract manufacturer based in Laguna Hills, California, with over 30 years of experience in the medical device industry. The company is ISO 13485-certified and operates an FDA-registered, HEPA-filtered facility, ensuring high standards in quality and compliance. A&M Biomedical specializes in the manual and electro-mechanical assembly of both sterile and non-sterile medical devices.

Founded in 1994, A&M Biomedical provides a full range of services, including process development, supply chain management, and quality documentation. The company produces various medical devices, such as respiratory, ophthalmic, and cardiac devices, as well as dental and surgical products. A&M Biomedical partners with medical device companies of all sizes, offering scalable production solutions that cater to both low and high-volume manufacturing needs. Their commitment to quality and reliability makes them a trusted partner in the medical device sector.

fields.notes:
Description: At Glint Lab, we are more than just a histopathology lab. We are a team of seasoned histologists and scientists dedicated to revolutionizing preclinical and early drug discovery through our comprehensive suite of services.

Experience Meets Expertise: Our team comprises experienced histologists and scientists with decades of collective experience. From human and animal tissues to xenograft tumors and frozen samples, we specialize in delivering top-notch histopathology solutions tailored to your unique research needs.

Quality Beyond Measure: Glint Lab is synonymous with quality, from meticulous tissue processing to cutting-edge histochemical staining and whole-slide imaging. Our commitment ensures every project delivers high-caliber data.

Collaboration at Every Step: We collaborate closely from study inception to completion, guiding tissue fixation methods, selecting suitable antibodies, and aligning project goals with your inquiries. We provide comprehensive reports and expert consultations to plan next steps.

Responsive and Friendly: Our skilled team values communication, offering consistent support and guidance on study design and project milestones.

A Partnership, Not Just a Service: We view our relationship with clients as a partnership, extending beyond transactional services to foster collaborations that drive scientific discovery. Your success fuels our commitment to delivering the best data possible.

fields.name: Innoviva
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 192500000; Latest funding: Convertible Note (192500000) on 2017-08-01
fields.notes:
Description: Innoviva is a biopharmaceutical holding company based in Burlingame, California. It focuses on managing and investing in a diverse portfolio of biopharmaceutical assets, primarily in respiratory, infectious disease, and critical care therapies. The company operates on an asset-light model, generating revenue mainly through royalty income from global sales of respiratory medicines developed in partnership with GlaxoSmithKline.

Innoviva has three main divisions: Respiratory Assets, which includes long-acting therapies for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and asthma; a Critical Care and Infectious Disease Platform, featuring a range of marketed products and late-stage therapies; and Subsidiary Operations, which involve strategic investments in specialized therapeutic areas. Its subsidiary, Innoviva Specialty Therapeutics, develops therapies for serious bacterial infections and critical care, while Entasis Therapeutics focuses on novel antibiotics for multidrug-resistant infections. The company is committed to addressing high unmet medical needs in healthcare.

fields.name: Meru Vasimmune
fields.address: 3550 General Atomics Ct, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-1194
fields.notes:
Description: Meru VasImmune is at the forefront of innovation in thrombosis research, providing advanced solutions to accelerate drug development and improve patient outcomes. Our Contract Research Organization (CRO) services offer end-to-end support, including cutting-edge in vitro assays, ex vivo models, and unique in vivo systems tailored to uncover critical insights into platelet biology and vascular diseases.

With a commitment to precision, innovation, and collaboration, we empower pharmaceutical and biotech companies to develop effective antiplatelet therapies and vascular interventions. At Meru VasImmune, we combine scientific rigor with customized approaches, ensuring that every partnership drives meaningful progress in addressing unmet medical needs.

Let's shape the future of thrombosis research together. #MeruVasImmune #ThrombosisResearch #InnovativeScience

fields.name: Neuroservices-Alliance
fields.address: Le Puy-Sainte-Reparade, Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur, France, 13610
fields.notes:
Description: Neuroservices-Alliance is a specialized Contract Research Organization (CRO) focused on drug discovery for the Central Nervous System (CNS) and Pain sectors. Founded in 2018 and based in Le Puy-Sainte-Réparade, France, the company serves the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries globally. It operates as a consortium of expert neuroscientists, providing high-quality, translational data to support research programs.

The company offers a range of services, including translational electrophysiology and behavioral testing, with both in vitro and in vivo solutions. Their capabilities include advanced techniques such as Multi-Electrode Array (MEA), Patch-Clamp, and optogenetics, utilizing various tissue models from rodents to human-derived neurons. Neuroservices-Alliance emphasizes customized protocols and direct communication with clients to meet specific research goals, ensuring flexibility and responsiveness in their operations.

fields.name: Wax-It Histology Services
fields.address: 2386 East Mall, Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada, V6T 1Z3
fields.notes:
Description: Wax-it Histology Services Inc. is a privately owned Contract Research Organization (CRO) based in Vancouver, Canada. Founded in 1994 and revived in 2003, the company specializes in preclinical histopathology services. It operates from the University of British Columbia and has completed over 14,100 projects for more than 600 clients, achieving a 99% customer retention rate.

The company offers a wide range of histology services, including general and specialized histology, immunohistochemistry, immunofluorescence, and digital pathology. Wax-it emphasizes quality through rigorous quality control on every slide and utilizes state-of-the-art technology. With a focus on flexibility and a client-centric approach, Wax-it supports various scientific endeavors, including preclinical toxicology and biomarker discovery. Over 80% of its clients are based in the USA, reflecting its global reach in biotechnology research.

fields.notes:
Description: Molecular Devices is a prominent American company based in San Jose, California, specializing in bioanalytical measurement systems, software, and consumables for life science research, pharmaceutical development, and biotherapeutic discovery. Founded in 1983, it became an operating subsidiary of Danaher Corporation in 2010, enhancing its resources and global reach. The company employs around 1,200 associates and operates in key markets including the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Korea, China, and Japan.

Molecular Devices offers a wide range of services and products designed to support automated workflows in life sciences. Their offerings include automated workflow solutions, high-throughput screening platforms, genomic and cellular analysis tools, and advanced imaging systems. The product portfolio features microplate readers, automated cellular imaging systems, and specialized software for data analysis. Molecular Devices serves a diverse clientele, including academic institutions, pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and government agencies, contributing to significant advancements in areas such as cancer therapy and infectious disease research.

raw_data.Short Description:
Molecular Devices is a prominent American company based in San Jose, California, specializing in bioanalytical measurement systems, software, and consumables for life science research, pharmaceutical development, and biotherapeutic discovery. Founded in 1983, it became an operating subsidiary of Danaher Corporation in 2010, enhancing its resources and global reach. The company employs around 1,200 associates and operates in key markets including the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, Korea, China, and Japan.

Molecular Devices offers a wide range of services and products designed to support automated workflows in life sciences. Their offerings include automated workflow solutions, high-throughput screening platforms, genomic and cellular analysis tools, and advanced imaging systems. The product portfolio features microplate readers, automated cellular imaging systems, and specialized software for data analysis. Molecular Devices serves a diverse clientele, including academic institutions, pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and government agencies, contributing to significant advancements in areas such as cancer therapy and infectious disease research.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 200000000; Latest funding: Venture (Round not Specified) (200000000) on 2024-09-01
fields.notes:
Description: At Serán, Our mission is to provide optimized drug development and manufacturing services for our clients, from discovery to the clinic.  Our science-driven approach utilizes predictive design tools, novel chemistry, analytical tools, enabling delivery technologies, and a thorough knowledge of quality and regulatory requirements to guide our customers on their clinical development path.  
Serán's team are experts in delivery of poorly soluble compounds. Serán employs a range of novel technologies and pharmaceutical manufacturing operations to overcome drug development challenges for both small and large molecules, including complex granulations, spray-dried dispersions, hot melt extrudates, IR and MR tablet technologies, multiparticulates, and engineered particles for inhalation. 

Our custom-built spray driers enable a wide variety of particle formation, from nano-structures to micro-particulates. Our particle engineering approaches are combined with our broad expertise in solid dosage forms resulting in the optimum clinical trial materials for your drug, including suspensions, capsules, and tablets.

fields.notes:
Description: SK pharmteco is a global contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) and analytical services provider. Based in Rancho Cordova, California, the company specializes in producing active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), advanced intermediates, and cell and gene therapies for the pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors. As a subsidiary of SK Inc., part of the SK Group, SK pharmteco benefits from 80 years of industry experience and a robust global business network.

The company operates seven manufacturing and development facilities across the United States, South Korea, Ireland, and France. Its services encompass the entire drug development lifecycle, including process development, analytical testing, and commercial production. SK pharmteco focuses on small molecules, peptides, and viral vectors, supporting clients in developing treatments for various conditions, including cancer and cardiovascular diseases. The company is committed to secure management of customer intellectual property, fostering trust and reliability in its partnerships.

fields.name: ChemPartner
fields.address: Shanghai, Shanghai, China
fields.notes:
Description: ChemPartner is a global, full-service Contract Research Organization (CRO) and Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Shanghai, China. Founded in 2002, the company has over 20 years of experience and serves tens of thousands of clients worldwide. ChemPartner provides comprehensive solutions that support drug programs from early discovery through to Investigational New Drug (IND) submission and clinical trials.

The company offers a wide range of integrated services, including discovery biology, pharmacology, medicinal chemistry, and regulatory support for both small and large molecules. ChemPartner also specializes in biologics, antibody-drug conjugates, and microbiome-based healthcare solutions. With a global presence that includes facilities across five continents, ChemPartner primarily serves mid- to large-sized pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and academic institutions, focusing on major therapeutic areas such as oncology, immunology, and infectious diseases.

ChemPartner also specializes in biologics, antibody-drug conjugates, and microbiome-based healthcare solutions. With a global presence that includes facilities across five continents, ChemPartner primarily serves mid- to large-sized pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, and academic institutions, focusing on major therapeutic areas such as oncology, immunology, and infectious diseases.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 30090000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2021-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: BioAgilytix, officially known as BioAgilytix Labs, LLC, is a prominent global contract research organization (CRO) that specializes in bioanalytical testing solutions. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Durham, North Carolina, the company has established itself as a trusted partner for leading pharmaceutical and biotechnology firms worldwide. With a network of laboratory facilities totaling approximately 89,109 square feet, BioAgilytix operates in the United States, Europe, and Australia.

The company offers comprehensive bioanalytical support for all phases of drug development, including pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity testing, biomarker bioanalysis, and cell-based assays. BioAgilytix adheres to rigorous quality standards, including GLP, GCP, and GMP, ensuring data integrity and regulatory compliance. Their services are essential for the development, testing, and regulatory approval of therapeutics, making them a key player in the healthcare industry.

fields.name: Electra Therapeutics
fields.address: 681 Gateway Blvd, South San Francisco, California, United States, 94080
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 283000000; Latest funding: Series C (183000000) on 2025-10-01
fields.notes:
Description: Electra Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company based in South San Francisco, California, founded in 2018. The company specializes in developing first-in-class therapies that target Signal Regulatory Protein (SIRP) receptors to treat immunological diseases and cancer. Electra is currently in the late clinical stage, with one program in pivotal clinical development and two additional preclinical programs.

The company's lead product candidate, ELA026, is a monoclonal antibody designed to selectively deplete pathological immune cells while preserving normal immune functions. It is in pivotal development for secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (sHLH) and is also being evaluated for T Cell Lymphoma. Electra's innovative approach utilizes SIRP-directed antibodies to address the underlying pathology of various diseases. The company is committed to improving care for patients with unmet medical needs, particularly those suffering from overlooked diseases.

fields.address: Minato City, Tokyo, Japan, 105-0023
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 120000000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (120000000) on 2025-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: CMIC Group is Japan's first and largest Contract Research Organization (CRO), established in 1992 and headquartered in Minato-ku, Tokyo. With approximately 7,800 employees, the company operates globally, including locations in the United States and broader Asia. CMIC Group is publicly traded on the Tokyo Stock Exchange under the symbol 2309.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services throughout the drug development lifecycle. This includes clinical development, regulatory consulting, and data management, as well as manufacturing services under Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). CMIC Group also provides market access solutions, site support management, and specialized services for medical device manufacturers. Its unique "Pharmaceutical Value Creator" model allows for integrated support, catering to a diverse clientele that includes major pharmaceutical firms, biotechnology startups, academic institutions, and government entities.

raw_data.Short Description:
CMIC Group is Japan's first and largest Contract Research Organization (CRO), established in 1992 and headquartered in Minato-ku, Tokyo. With approximately 7,800 employees, the company operates globally, including locations in the United States and broader Asia. CMIC Group is publicly traded on the Tokyo Stock Exchange under the symbol 2309.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services throughout the drug development lifecycle. This includes clinical development, regulatory consulting, and data management, as well as manufacturing services under Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). CMIC Group also provides market access solutions, site support management, and specialized services for medical device manufacturers. Its unique "Pharmaceutical Value Creator" model allows for integrated support, catering to a diverse clientele that includes major pharmaceutical firms, biotechnology startups, academic institutions, and government entities.

fields.notes:
Description: Nexture Bio delivers animal-component-free microcarriers, scaffolds, cell-attachment factors, and scientific services to support scalable adherent cell culture across life sciences, food, and personal care. Our solutions replace animal-based and synthetic ingredients with biodegradable, plant-based alternatives that are safe and suitable for use in food.

fields.name: SoCal Biosciences
fields.address: 8535 Arjons Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92126-4360
fields.notes:
Description: SoCal Biosciences, Inc. is a San Diego-based Contract Research Organization (CRO) that specializes in bioanalytical services for advanced therapeutics, particularly RNA-based platforms. Founded by Dr. Patty Limphong, the company leverages over 20 years of experience in drug discovery and development. SoCal Biosciences focuses on delivering high-quality data and efficient service to support all phases of drug development, from candidate screening to regulatory submissions.

The company offers a range of bioanalytical services, including assays for RNA, proteins, and lipids, as well as biodistribution studies and pharmacokinetic assessments. SoCal Biosciences is recognized for its expertise in mRNA drug development and has contributed to significant regulatory milestones, including the submission for KOSTAIVE®, the first commercialized sa-mRNA COVID-19 vaccine. With a commitment to quality and collaboration, SoCal Biosciences positions itself as a trusted partner in the biotech and pharmaceutical sectors.

fields.name: V2 Clinical
fields.address: San Diego, California, United States
fields.notes:
Description: V2 Clinical is a next-generation consulting firm, redefining how clinical trials operate. With a deep understanding of both the sponsor and site perspectives, we provide flexible, high-performance solutions that drive efficiency, quality, and cost-effectiveness in clinical development.

Our team brings 20+ years of expertise in pharma and biotech, offering tailored services that align with your study's unique needs. We specialize in outsourcing, clinical site budgets & contracts, trial management, and operational strategy, ensuring seamless execution while fostering strong relationships.

What Sets Us Apart?
-Client-Centric Approach: We prioritize collaboration, transparency, and responsiveness.
-Industry Expertise: Led by seasoned professionals with buy- and sell-side experience.
-Efficient Negotiation Strategies: We streamline negotiations to balance site, vendor and sponsor goals.
-Innovative Solutions: Our proprietary and validated AI/ML-driven modeling accelerates drug discovery and precision medicine.

At V2 Clinical, we are committed to helping sponsors and CROs reduce trial timelines, control costs, and optimize site engagement.

Let's shape the future of clinical research. Connect with us today to explore how we can support your next trial.

fields.notes:
Description: Tecan Group Ltd. is a Swiss company based in Männedorf, specializing in laboratory automation. Founded in 1980, Tecan has grown to employ over 3,500 people worldwide. The company operates production, research, and development sites across North America, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region, with a sales and service network in 52 countries.

Tecan offers a wide range of advanced instruments and solutions for various sectors, including biopharmaceuticals, clinical diagnostics, forensics, and medical technology. Their core services include liquid handling automation, microplate readers, and software solutions for laboratory processes. As an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM), Tecan also develops customized instruments and components for partner companies. Their products are designed to enhance precision and compliance in laboratory workflows, supporting clients in drug discovery, diagnostics, and academic research.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 310000; Latest funding: Other (310000) on 2018-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: OZ Biosciences is a biotechnology research company founded in 2003 and based in Marseille, France, with a branch in San Diego, California. The company specializes in developing and commercializing molecular delivery systems, particularly focused on transfection and transduction tools for the scientific community and pharmaceutical industries. With a team of around 20 employees, OZ Biosciences aims to support scientists in accelerating discoveries through advanced technological tools.

The company offers a range of proprietary technologies, including Magnetofection™, Magneto-Biolistic, Lipofection, and Polyfection, among others. Their product line features laboratory reagents for both in vitro and in vivo applications, such as transfection reagents, virology and stem cell reagents, vectors, vaccine adjuvants, and assay kits. OZ Biosciences also provides custom services, including custom transfection and mRNA production, catering to various research fields like neurosciences, regenerative medicine, and cancer research.

raw_data.Short Description:
OZ Biosciences is a biotechnology research company founded in 2003 and based in Marseille, France, with a branch in San Diego, California. The company specializes in developing and commercializing molecular delivery systems, particularly focused on transfection and transduction tools for the scientific community and pharmaceutical industries. With a team of around 20 employees, OZ Biosciences aims to support scientists in accelerating discoveries through advanced technological tools.

The company offers a range of proprietary technologies, including Magnetofection™, Magneto-Biolistic, Lipofection, and Polyfection, among others. Their product line features laboratory reagents for both in vitro and in vivo applications, such as transfection reagents, virology and stem cell reagents, vectors, vaccine adjuvants, and assay kits. OZ Biosciences also provides custom services, including custom transfection and mRNA production, catering to various research fields like neurosciences, regenerative medicine, and cancer research.
raw_data.Product Category: Transfection, delivery, immunotherapy, LNP/mRNA, and molecular-biology reagents

fields.name: AbTherx
fields.notes:
Description: AbTherx, Inc. is a biotechnology company based in Mountain View, California, founded in 2023. The company specializes in innovative transgenic mouse technologies aimed at therapeutic antibody discovery. Its flagship platform, Atlas™ Mice, generates diverse human antibody sequences to target challenging proteins like GPCRs and ion channels. With a leadership team that has extensive experience in over 1,000 discovery campaigns and 13 marketed therapeutics, AbTherx is well-positioned to advance medicine.

The Atlas™ Mice platform includes transgenic mice that express full human antibody diversity, enabling the development of monoclonal and bispecific antibodies. AbTherx also integrates technologies like ActivMAb® to enhance drug discovery for complex targets. The company offers flexible services, including technology licensing and research collaborations, catering to both startups and large pharmaceutical companies. AbTherx has initiated over 50 programs with more than 25 partners, demonstrating its commitment to resource efficiency and effective timelines in the biotechnology sector.

fields.name: 10x Genomics

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 812330000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (51430000) on 2020-10-01
fields.notes:
Description: 10x Genomics is a life science technology company based in Pleasanton, California, founded in 2012. The company specializes in designing and manufacturing advanced sequencing technologies for single-cell and spatial omics research. Its mission is to create products that help scientists analyze biological systems at the resolution of individual cells and tissue structures. 10x Genomics is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol TXG and has a global presence with offices in the Netherlands, Taiwan, China, Singapore, and Japan.

The company offers three main platforms: Chromium, Visium, and Xenium. These platforms provide integrated instruments, microfluidic chips, and analysis software for various applications, including transcriptomic profiling, spatial gene expression, and in situ single-cell imaging. 10x Genomics serves academic researchers, translational researchers, and biopharmaceutical companies, contributing to advancements in fields such as oncology, immunology, and neuroscience. With a workforce of approximately 1,310 employees, 10x Genomics is committed to expanding its product offerings and enhancing human health through innovative research solutions.

fields.notes:
Description: Agilent Technologies, Inc. is a global leader based in Santa Clara, California, specializing in instruments, software, services, and consumables for laboratories. Founded in 1999 as a spin-off from Hewlett-Packard, Agilent focuses on advancing life science research, patient diagnostics, and ensuring the safety of water, food, and pharmaceuticals. The company operates in 110 countries and employs approximately 18,000 people.

Agilent's business is organized into three main divisions: Life Sciences & Applied Markets, Diagnostics & Genomics, and Agilent CrossLab. These divisions provide tailored solutions, including advanced analytical tools for chromatography, spectroscopy, and lab management services. Agilent serves a diverse customer base across key industries such as biopharmaceuticals, environmental testing, and food safety, generating significant sales from these sectors. The company is recognized for its commitment to quality and integrity in its mission to enhance the quality of life.

raw_data.Short Description:
Agilent Technologies, Inc. is a global leader based in Santa Clara, California, specializing in instruments, software, services, and consumables for laboratories. Founded in 1999 as a spin-off from Hewlett-Packard, Agilent focuses on advancing life science research, patient diagnostics, and ensuring the safety of water, food, and pharmaceuticals. The company operates in 110 countries and employs approximately 18,000 people.

Agilent's business is organized into three main divisions: Life Sciences & Applied Markets, Diagnostics & Genomics, and Agilent CrossLab. These divisions provide tailored solutions, including advanced analytical tools for chromatography, spectroscopy, and lab management services. Agilent serves a diverse customer base across key industries such as biopharmaceuticals, environmental testing, and food safety, generating significant sales from these sectors. The company is recognized for its commitment to quality and integrity in its mission to enhance the quality of life.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 1478500000; Latest funding: Debt Financing (850000000) on 2026-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc. is a global biotechnology company based in San Rafael, California. Founded in 1997, it focuses on developing and commercializing innovative therapies for serious, life-threatening rare genetic diseases. The company specializes in enzyme replacement therapies, gene therapies, and other advanced treatments that target the root causes of genetically defined conditions. BioMarin has a strong clinical pipeline and a proven track record, with nine commercial therapies available.

The company's core products address rare inherited disorders, including mucopolysaccharidosis types I, IV, and VI, phenylketonuria, achondroplasia, severe hemophilia A, and neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis type 2. Key therapies include Vimizim, Palynziq, Brineura, Kuvan, Naglazyme, and Voxzogo. BioMarin distributes its products through specialty pharmacies, hospitals, and authorized distributors across various regions, including the Americas, Europe, and Asia. With approximately 3,040 employees, BioMarin is committed to expanding its pipeline of next-generation therapies under the leadership of President and CEO Alexander Hardy.

fields.name: Just - Evotec Biologics
fields.address: 7 Essener Bogen, Hamburg, Hamburg, Germany, 22419
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 121000000; Latest funding: Other on 2020-10-01
fields.notes:
Description: Just – Evotec Biologics is a subsidiary of Evotec SE, based in Seattle, Washington. The company specializes in biologics development and manufacturing, focusing on accelerating the creation of biotherapeutics while reducing costs. Founded in 2014 as Just Biotherapeutics, it became part of Evotec in 2019, enhancing its capabilities with a strong team and advanced facilities.

The company offers comprehensive services in biologics discovery, development, and biomanufacturing. Its key strengths include AI-driven molecular design, cell line development, and continuous manufacturing strategies. Just – Evotec Biologics emphasizes innovative solutions for protein therapeutics, including monoclonal antibodies and biosimilars, supporting projects from initial discovery through to market supply. The company leverages its proprietary J.DESIGN platform to streamline processes and improve efficiency in drug development.

fields.name: Aurion Biotech
fields.address: 1200 6th Avenue, Seattle, Washington, United States, 98101
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 146055618; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2025-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Aurion Biotech is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing innovative regenerative cell therapies to restore vision in patients with corneal endothelial diseases, which are significant causes of blindness. Founded in 2021 and headquartered in Seattle, Washington, with additional offices in Boston and Tokyo, the company aims to provide accessible and effective therapies for millions of patients worldwide.

The company's lead product, Vyznova™, is the first allogeneic cell therapy approved for treating corneal edema due to endothelial dysfunction, receiving global regulatory approval in Japan in March 2023. Aurion Biotech is also advancing AURN001, a next-generation candidate currently in Phase 1/2 clinical trials in the U.S. and Canada. With a proprietary process for culturing healthy donor corneal cells, Aurion Biotech is dedicated to transforming the treatment landscape for ocular diseases and reducing reliance on traditional donor corneal transplants.

Description: Applied StemCell (ASC) is a genome engineering platform company advancing the future of genetic and cell-based medicine through its proprietary TARGATT™ and S-SELeCT™ technologies. These innovations enable safe, scalable, site-specific integration of large payloads with reliable gene expression for the development of next-generation therapies, ex vivo and in vivo. 

With deep expertise in iPSCs and cell line engineering, ASC provides end-to-end solutions that accelerate innovation across academia and biopharma. Our strong IP foundation and cutting-edge technologies empower partners to move from concept to clinic with precision, scalability, and confidence. appliedstemcell.com

raw_data.Short Description:
Applied StemCell (ASC) is a genome engineering platform company advancing the future of genetic and cell-based medicine through its proprietary TARGATT™ and S-SELeCT™ technologies. These innovations enable safe, scalable, site-specific integration of large payloads with reliable gene expression for the development of next-generation therapies, ex vivo and in vivo. 

With deep expertise in iPSCs and cell line engineering, ASC provides end-to-end solutions that accelerate innovation across academia and biopharma. Our strong IP foundation and cutting-edge technologies empower partners to move from concept to clinic with precision, scalability, and confidence. appliedstemcell.com

fields.name: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals
fields.address: 6055 Lusk Blvd, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-2700
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 10871700028; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (10000000000) on 2026-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2008 and publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker CRNX since 2018, Crinetics focuses on developing innovative, orally delivered therapies for patients with rare endocrine diseases and endocrine-related tumors. The company specializes in drug discovery targeting G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs), particularly peptide hormone receptors, to create small-molecule therapeutics that meet significant medical needs.

The company has a strong pipeline with over 10 programs, including its flagship product, Paltusotine (marketed as PALSONIFY), which is the first oral SST2 agonist approved for treating acromegaly. Other candidates in development address conditions such as congenital adrenal hyperplasia, Cushing’s syndrome, and various endocrine disorders. Crinetics is committed to empowering patients and seeks strategic partnerships to advance its therapeutic programs into successful products.

fields.name: Active Motif
fields.address: 1914 Palomar Oaks Way, Carlsbad, California, United States, 92008-6509
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 4173552; Latest funding: Other on 2021-09-01
fields.notes:
Description: Active Motif is a global biotechnology company based in Carlsbad, California, specializing in research tools for epigenetics and gene regulation. Founded in 1999, the company is dedicated to empowering researchers in understanding cellular processes and disease mechanisms. Active Motif provides high-quality products and exceptional support to the life science, clinical, pharmaceutical, and drug discovery sectors.

The company offers a wide range of services, including custom contract research, bioinformatics applications, technical support, custom assay development, and training resources. Their extensive product portfolio features core research reagents like antibodies, chromatin modifiers, and recombinant proteins, as well as kits for sample preparation, ChIP assays, and DNA methylation analysis. Active Motif also develops advanced tools such as biocomputing systems and fluorescence detection systems to aid in gene expression studies.

Active Motif serves a diverse clientele, including academic institutions, biotechnology and pharmaceutical companies, clinical research organizations, and reference laboratories. With regional offices in Belgium, Japan, and China, the company maintains a strong global presence, ensuring accessibility to its innovative research solutions.

fields.name: Curavit
fields.address: Boston, Massachusetts, United States
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 16426701; Latest funding: Series A (5000000) on 2022-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: Curavit Clinical Research, founded in 2020, is the world's first all-virtual Contract Research Organization (VCRO). Based in Scarsdale, New York, with operational hubs in Boston, New York, Salt Lake City, and San Diego, Curavit focuses on decentralized clinical trials (DCTs) that prioritize patient accessibility and engagement. The company utilizes advanced technologies such as digital health, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence to streamline the clinical trial process, allowing patients to participate from their homes or local clinics.

Curavit offers a comprehensive suite of services, including decentralized trial protocol design, virtual clinical site operations, patient recruitment, data management, and regulatory support. Its proprietary STRATUS platform serves as a central hub for trial activities, enabling real-time collaboration and data capture. By targeting pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology firms, healthcare organizations, and medical device manufacturers, Curavit aims to enhance trial diversity and data quality while reducing operational burdens for its clients.

fields.name: MP Biomedicals
fields.address: 6 Thomas, Irvine, California, United States, 92618-2500
fields.notes:
Description: MP Biomedicals is a global life sciences company based in Irvine, California, with a strong presence in the United States, Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. Founded in 1959, the company has over 65 years of experience in serving research, industrial, and clinical communities. As a wholly-owned subsidiary of Valiant Company, MP Biomedicals employs approximately 500 to 1,000 people and is known for its extensive portfolio that includes life sciences products, fine chemicals, and diagnostics.

The company offers a wide range of services, including research support, custom manufacturing, quality control, technical training, and logistics. MP Biomedicals manufactures and distributes over 55,000 products, such as reagents, kits, diagnostic tools, and specialty chemicals. Its facilities are ISO-certified and FDA-approved, ensuring compliance with high-quality standards. With a mission to empower scientists and researchers, MP Biomedicals aims to be a leading provider of integrated life science solutions, driving scientific excellence and advancing healthcare globally.

Description: At Pacific Vial, we specialize in the manufacturing of premium glass vials tailored to meet the needs of industries ranging from pharmaceuticals to cosmetics. With over two decades of experience, we deliver high-quality, durable, and customizable glass vials that offer unmatched precision, safety, and aesthetic appeal.

We take pride in being an ISO 9001:2015 certified company, ensuring that every product meets stringent quality standards. Our team works closely with clients to create tailored solutions, whether it's custom vial designs, neck finishes, or specialized treatments like delamination prevention and internal siliconization.

Our mission is to provide the best glass vial solutions for our clients, with a focus on quality, innovation, and exceptional service. We're committed to supporting industries with reliable and versatile packaging solutions while maintaining our dedication to sustainability and environmental responsibility.

Explore our full range of glass vial solutions at www.pacificvial.com or contact our sales team to discuss how we can help meet your packaging needs.

Description: Credence MedSystems is an industry-recognized and award-winning innovator, addressing unmet needs in injectable drug delivery. With infrastructure and competencies to design, develop, and commercialize new products, we enable our Pharma and Biotech partners to bring new therapies to patients across the world.

Guided by our Innovation Without Change® philosophy, Credence develops solutions that enhance safety, usability, and performance while integrating seamlessly into existing workflows and manufacturing processes. Our core product platforms include the Companion® prefilled syringes, Dual Chamber delivery systems, and Metered-Dosing technologies, supporting a broad range of therapeutic applications.
 
By reducing development risk, accelerating time-to-market, and improving experiences for patients, caregivers, and healthcare providers, Credence helps our pharmaceutical and biotechnology partners bring life changing injectable therapies to market with confidence.

fields.name: Access Biologicals
fields.address: 995 Park Center Drive, Vista, California, United States, 92081
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 147400000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (147400000) on 2023-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Access Biologicals was acquired by Grifols. Visit the Grifols Bio Supplies website for all your human biological product needs: www.biosuppliesproducts.com.

Access Biologicals LLC, a subsidiary of Grifols, is an industry leader in the collection and manufacturing of an extensive portfolio of biological products. Combined with closed-loop material sourcing, we can provide critical support for various markets such as in-vitro diagnostic manufacturing, biopharmaceutical, cell culture and diagnostic research and development. Viral testing and donor examinations are performed following current State, CLIA and FDA regulations allowing us to deliver consistently high-quality human blood products. Manufacturing capabilities include a variety of plasma and serum processing in fully auditable facilities with on-site monitored storage. Our high throughput laboratory provides customer-driven responsiveness to specifications and product requirements. Our cell culture division offers a variety of human sera products and is the only company to directly collect and process Human AB serum at FDA-licensed facilities located within the US.

Saturn Biomedical, our state-of-the-art plasma and blood collection center in Indianapolis, IN, is uniquely positioned as the largest and newest speciality donor center in the United States.

FDA/CLIA/ISO licenses

FDA Registration # 307234900 – Access Manufacturing
FDA Registration # 3006118943 – Access Biologicals
CLIA Certification ID # 15D0891286 – Access Plasma dba Saturn Bio
FDA License # 1193 – Access Plasma dba Saturn Bio
ISO 9001 Certified

fields.name: Bio-Edge
fields.address: 6369 Nancy Ridge Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-6206
fields.notes:
Description: Bio-Edge, Inc. provides innovative solutions for polymer and material applications. Since our founding in 1993, we have diversified into medical devices, amphiphilic polymers, polymeric membranes, and polymeric controlled release of small molecules.

In addition to custom synthesis, Bio-Edge provides cGMP chemical process development, pilot-scale, and manufacturing services to its customers. Our 7200 square foot facility is equipped with jacketed chemical reactors that range in volume from 250ml to 50L with temperature control capabilities from less than -50°C to greater than 200°C. Bio-Edge has served clients that include both large global companies and smaller start-ups. Our development services target organizations that do not have in-house R&D or pilot-scale chemical manufacturing capabilities and desire an easy-to-work-with flexible partner. Customers come to BE to improve their chances for success.

Keywords: chemical manufacturing, chemicals

Technologies: Gmail, Google Apps, Amazon AWS, Vimeo, Nginx, Google Analytics

fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Convertible Note (0) on 2020-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: Dalton Bioanalytics is at the forefront of multiomics revolution, providing advanced CRO services for proteomics, metabolomics, lipidomics, and exposomics. Our proprietary Omni-MS® platform is a paradigm shift, enabling the analysis of over 1000+ biochemicals in a single sample—a feat previously considered infeasible. We are dedicated to resolving the inefficiency and expense of traditional laboratory testing by digitizing the complete biochemical composition of biofloud samples. This creates a comprehensive digital signature that translates into a wealth of actionable health insights, accelerating drug discovery and biomarker research for our clients. Our commitment to innovation is honored by our namesake, John Dalton, the father of modern atomic theory, reflecting our reliance on advanced mass spectrometry.

fields.notes:
Description: EZ Labs LLC is a privately owned cGMP compliant analytical laboratory in the heart of San Diego's thriving biotech community. Our team specializes in solving complex analytical issues faced by biotech and pharma companies, offering end-to-end analytical services from pre-clinical stage to NDA filing. Our 8,000 square-foot facility is designed for comprehensive analytical capabilities, enabling us to deliver breakthrough solutions for your analytical development needs. 

Whether you're a biotech startup or an established player in pharma, EZ Labs' mission is to make the analytical process easy, collaborative, and successful. We go beyond the ordinary by setting high standards and expectations to ensure that every project is handled with care and precision. 

Founded in 2016 by Anna Eidelman and Irina Zamansky, EZ Labs possesses an outstanding reputation in the biotech community, bringing their high level of expertise in CMC development for biotech startups and small to mid-sized pharma companies lacking internal analytical capabilities.

Follow the link to our website to learn more about our services!

Whether you're a biotech startup or an established player in pharma, EZ Labs' mission is to make the analytical process easy, collaborative, and successful. We go beyond the ordinary by setting high standards and expectations to ensure that every project is handled with care and precision. 

Founded in 2016 by Anna Eidelman and Irina Zamansky, EZ Labs possesses an outstanding reputation in the biotech community, bringing their high level of expertise in CMC development for biotech startups and small to mid-sized pharma companies lacking internal analytical capabilities.

Follow the link to our website to learn more about our services!

fields.name: iQ Biosciences
fields.address: 1640 S Loop Rd, Alameda, California, United States, 94502
fields.notes:
Description: iQ Biosciences is a biotechnology company that specializes in providing high-quality biospecimens, in vitro bioservices, and bioconjugation solutions. Their focus is on supporting research in immunology, drug discovery, and immuno-oncology for the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, diagnostic, and academic sectors. The company emphasizes core values of Intelligence, Quality, and Empathy, positioning itself as a trusted partner in the biomedical community.

The company offers a range of products, including primary cells from human and non-human sources, such as Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cells (PBMCs). These biospecimens are essential for studying various health conditions, including cancer and autoimmune disorders. iQ Biosciences also provides intelligent in vitro bioservices, including assays for cytokine release, hemolysis, immune monitoring, and antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity. Their services are designed to support the development of immuno-oncology therapeutics, utilizing the immune system to combat cancer.

fields.name: Mediconomics
fields.address: 81B Misburger Strasse, Hanover, Lower Saxony, Germany, 30625
fields.notes:
Description: Mediconomics GmbH is a full-service Contract Research Organization (CRO) and pharmaceutical consultant based in Hannover, Germany. Founded in 1997, the company specializes in the development, testing, and regulatory approval of medicinal products and medical devices. With over 25 years of experience, Mediconomics operates internationally, with branches in Denmark, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, and the United States, ensuring high-quality standards and compliance with regulatory frameworks.

The company offers a wide range of services, including clinical trial management, regulatory affairs, data management, pharmacovigilance, medical writing, and product development consulting. Mediconomics supports clients across various sectors, including pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, and medical devices, helping them navigate complex regulatory environments and accelerate market entry. With a commitment to quality and compliance, Mediconomics is certified under ISO 9001:2015 and adheres to international guidelines, providing clients with reliable and efficient solutions for their research and development needs.

fields.name: Novotech, A Global Clinical Research Organization
fields.address: 9 Raffles Pl, Singapore, Singapore, Singapore, 04
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 760000000; Latest funding: Venture (Round not Specified) (0) on 2025-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Novotech is a full-service clinical research organization (CRO) and scientific advisory partner, founded in 1997. Headquartered in Singapore, with a significant presence in Sydney, Australia, the company specializes in accelerating drug development for biotech and small- to mid-sized pharmaceutical companies. Novotech operates over 30 offices across Asia-Pacific, North America, and Europe, employing around 2,800 to 3,000 professionals globally.

The company offers comprehensive clinical development services that cover the entire trial lifecycle, from Phase I to Phase IV. Its services include clinical trials, bioequivalence studies, patient recruitment, drug development consulting, and regulatory expertise. Novotech has successfully conducted more than 5,000 clinical projects and has played a role in bringing over 60 drugs to market. It is recognized as Australia’s largest independent CRO and has received multiple awards for its contributions to the industry.

fields.address: 6160 Cornerstone Ct E, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-3710
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Other on 2020-09-01
fields.notes:
Description: ProSciento, Inc. is a specialized Clinical Research Organization (CRO) dedicated to the development of therapeutics for metabolic diseases. With over 20 years of experience, the company offers comprehensive clinical research and development services to pharmaceutical and biotechnology clients. ProSciento focuses on areas such as diabetes, obesity, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, having successfully executed more than 425 clinical trials.

Founded in 2003 and headquartered in San Diego, California, ProSciento provides a range of services including clinical trial design, patient engagement, regulatory affairs, and data management. The company is recognized for its innovative methodologies tailored to metabolic conditions and emphasizes its commitment to quality and scientific rigor. ProSciento serves global pharmaceutical companies, biotech firms, and academic institutions, positioning itself as a leader in the metabolic disease sector.

fields.name: Vanguard Clinical
fields.address: 12265 El Camino Real, San Diego, California, United States, 92130-2006
fields.notes:
Description: Healthcare is about people. That's why at Vanguard Clinical we work to build the best possible relationships with our clients and the medical professionals we engage on their behalf. Motivated investigators achieve faster enrollment with fewer screen failures and protocol deviations, saving our clients both time and money. We look forward to building a long lasting relationship with your organization.

With over 20 years of industry experience, Vanguard Clinical Inc. is a leader in clinical trial management. Our highly talented and diverse team of consultants has the expertise to carry out all phases of trials in many therapeutic areas.

At Vanguard Clinical, we understand that the right patient population can be anywhere. With experience conducting trials on five continents, we recognize the challenges and logistics unique to certain areas of the world. From regulations to training, Vanguard Clinical employs the optimal strategy to ensure trials are conducted consistently and return the best possible data.

A successful clinical trial can be a marathon, and Vanguard Clinical will be by your side the entire way. During startup, we can manage or assist with initial feasibility and budget forecasting, negotiating contracts with vendors and clinical sites, protocol writing, and Investigator on-boarding. As trials progress, we provide expert analysis to ensure resources are being utilized efficiently. We pride ourselves on quality results, integrity of data, and ongoing process development to ensure the success of the projects for our clients.

We strive to be innovators and are always refining our processes to provide a tight feedback loop between the monitors in the field and our clients, keeping them continuously informed about the status of a trial.

fields.name: Automated Systems of Tacoma
fields.product_category: Aseptic fill-finish, isolator, robotics, and packaging automation systems
fields.notes:
Description: AST, Inc., also known as Automated Systems of Tacoma, LLC, is a technology leader based in Tacoma, Washington. Founded in 1965, the company specializes in advanced aseptic filling and closing systems for the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries. With over 60 years of experience, AST focuses on American-made drug product manufacturing technology that meets stringent regulatory requirements for injectable therapies.

The company offers a range of flexible aseptic filling equipment, including the GENiSYS® Lab, GENiSYS C, and GENiSYS R systems, which cater to various production scales. Their ASEPTiCell® is notable for being the first multi-format cGMP aseptic fill-finish machine, capable of processing multiple container types using advanced automation. AST also provides custom automation solutions for sterile injectable applications, supporting the production of life-saving therapies. The company serves clients globally, primarily in the biotechnology and pharmaceutical sectors, and has recently partnered with the Marchesini Group to enhance its capabilities in aseptic fill-finish and pharmaceutical packaging.

fields.name: Gilead Sciences
fields.address: 333 Lakeside Drive, Foster City, California, United States, 94404
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 24038350000; Latest funding: Other on 2026-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: Gilead Sciences, Inc. is a prominent American biopharmaceutical company based in Foster City, California. Founded in 1987, Gilead focuses on discovering, developing, and commercializing innovative medicines for life-threatening diseases. The company specializes in three main therapeutic areas: virology, oncology, and inflammation. Its virology efforts address conditions such as HIV/AIDS and viral hepatitis, while oncology focuses on cancer treatments and cell therapy. Inflammation therapies target various conditions in rheumatology, pulmonology, dermatology, and gastroenterology.

With a workforce of approximately 14,000 to 18,000 employees across more than 35 countries, Gilead is committed to advancing therapies for unmet medical needs. The company offers a diverse portfolio of branded medicines, including treatments for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, and oncology, as well as therapies for respiratory and infectious diseases. Gilead markets its products to healthcare providers worldwide, aiming to improve care for patients facing serious health challenges.

raw_data.Short Description:
Gilead Sciences, Inc. is a prominent American biopharmaceutical company based in Foster City, California. Founded in 1987, Gilead focuses on discovering, developing, and commercializing innovative medicines for life-threatening diseases. The company specializes in three main therapeutic areas: virology, oncology, and inflammation. Its virology efforts address conditions such as HIV/AIDS and viral hepatitis, while oncology focuses on cancer treatments and cell therapy. Inflammation therapies target various conditions in rheumatology, pulmonology, dermatology, and gastroenterology.

With a workforce of approximately 14,000 to 18,000 employees across more than 35 countries, Gilead is committed to advancing therapies for unmet medical needs. The company offers a diverse portfolio of branded medicines, including treatments for HIV/AIDS, viral hepatitis, and oncology, as well as therapies for respiratory and infectious diseases. Gilead markets its products to healthcare providers worldwide, aiming to improve care for patients facing serious health challenges.

fields.name: Araceli Biosciences
fields.address: Portland, Oregon, United States
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 21425000; Latest funding: Seed (11200000) on 2025-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: Araceli Biosciences is transforming therapeutic discovery with technologies built for speed, precision, and impact. Our advanced High Content Imaging platforms deliver real-time analysis, giving researchers immediate, actionable answers during experiments. Responding at the pace of biology accelerates decision-making, improves candidate selection, and reduces costly downstream failures.
 
At the same time, our platforms generate high-quality, analysis-ready data that power AI-driven models and discovery pipelines. This dual advantage, instant insights today and predictive power tomorrow, makes Araceli a catalyst for faster, more confident therapeutic breakthroughs. We believe in a future where patients access the most effective treatments sooner, and Araceli is forging that future.

fields.name: AGC Biologics
fields.address: 22021 20th Ave SE, Bothell, Washington, United States, 98021
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 646290000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (461990000) on 2016-12-01
fields.notes:
Description: AGC Biologics is a global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Bothell, Washington. The company specializes in the development and commercial-scale manufacturing of protein-based biologics, cell and gene therapies, and messenger RNA (mRNA). With a network of cGMP-compliant facilities across the United States, Europe, and Asia, AGC Biologics serves over 100 customers worldwide, including biopharmaceutical companies, academic institutions, and vaccine developers.

Founded in 2001, AGC Biologics offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire biopharmaceutical lifecycle. Their capabilities include cell line development, upstream and downstream process development, analytical and formulation development, and commercial-scale manufacturing. The company also focuses on specialized therapies, producing cell therapies, gene therapies, and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). Backed by AGC, Inc., AGC Biologics combines extensive industry experience with a commitment to delivering high-quality solutions in the biotechnology and pharmaceutical manufacturing sectors.

fields.name: Quotient Sciences
fields.address: Nottingham, England, United Kingdom, NG11 6
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 35980000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2019-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Quotient Sciences is a global drug development and manufacturing accelerator, specializing as a Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRDMO). Founded in 1990 as a spin-out from the University of Nottingham, the company employs over 1,300 staff and operates advanced clinical and manufacturing facilities in the UK and the US. Quotient Sciences aims to help clients reduce the time and cost of bringing drugs to market.

The company offers a range of integrated services across the drug development pathway, utilizing its proprietary Translational Pharmaceutics® platform. This platform combines formulation development, real-time adaptive GMP manufacturing, and clinical pharmacology, which can shorten development timelines by an average of 12 months. Key services include drug product development and manufacturing, clinical pharmacology, drug substance synthesis, clinical trial manufacturing, and contract manufacturing. Quotient Sciences serves a diverse global client base, from emerging biotech firms to Fortune 500 pharmaceutical companies, and is committed to improving global health by transforming ideas into effective therapies.

raw_data.Short Description:
Quotient Sciences is a global drug development and manufacturing accelerator, specializing as a Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRDMO). Founded in 1990 as a spin-out from the University of Nottingham, the company employs over 1,300 staff and operates advanced clinical and manufacturing facilities in the UK and the US. Quotient Sciences aims to help clients reduce the time and cost of bringing drugs to market.

The company offers a range of integrated services across the drug development pathway, utilizing its proprietary Translational Pharmaceutics® platform. This platform combines formulation development, real-time adaptive GMP manufacturing, and clinical pharmacology, which can shorten development timelines by an average of 12 months. Key services include drug product development and manufacturing, clinical pharmacology, drug substance synthesis, clinical trial manufacturing, and contract manufacturing. Quotient Sciences serves a diverse global client base, from emerging biotech firms to Fortune 500 pharmaceutical companies, and is committed to improving global health by transforming ideas into effective therapies.

fields.name: Arcutis Biotherapeutics
fields.address: United States
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 896781250; Latest funding: Other (172500000) on 2024-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Arcutis Biotherapeutics, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative treatments for immune-mediated dermatological diseases. Founded in June 2016 and headquartered in Westlake Village, California, Arcutis aims to address unmet medical needs in dermatology with non-steroidal therapies. The company is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker ARQT.

Arcutis specializes in drug discovery and development, clinical program management, and regulatory strategy. Its lead product, ZORYVE, is an FDA-approved topical cream for plaque psoriasis and atopic dermatitis. The company also offers ZORYVE Foam for scalp and body psoriasis and seborrheic dermatitis. Arcutis is advancing a robust pipeline of clinical-stage products targeting various skin conditions, including hand eczema and alopecia areata, all designed to provide safer long-term treatment options. 

With a commitment to patient-centric innovation, Arcutis collaborates with dermatologists and healthcare systems to improve patient outcomes and redefine standards in dermatological care.

fields.name: CytomX Therapeutics
fields.address: 151 Oyster Point Boulevard, South San Francisco, California, United States, 94080
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 338500004; Latest funding: Other (30000000) on 2023-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: CytomX Therapeutics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company based in South San Francisco, California, focused on developing innovative cancer therapies. Founded in 2008, the company is known for its proprietary PROBODY® therapeutic platform, which creates "masked" antibody therapeutics that activate selectively within the tumor microenvironment. This technology aims to improve safety and efficacy compared to traditional antibody therapies.

CytomX's pipeline includes several clinical-stage candidates, such as Varsetatug Masetecan, a conditionally activated antibody-drug conjugate targeting EpCAM, and CX-801, a cytokine designed for solid tumors. The company collaborates with major pharmaceutical partners, including Amgen and Bristol Myers Squibb, to advance its research and development efforts. With a commitment to transforming cancer treatment, CytomX is dedicated to creating safer and more effective therapies for patients.

fields.address: 5717 Pacific Center Blvd, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-4250
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 1552500000; Latest funding: Other (1552500000) on 2020-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: Maravai brands set the standard in nucleic acid products and services, enzyme development, and biologics safety testing. From producing revolutionary mRNA technologies that drive infectious disease vaccines, cancer vaccines, and other cell and gene therapies to delivering gold-standard bioprocess impurity detection solutions, Maravai is helping life sciences companies overcome their biggest development and manufacturing challenges to streamline and scale from research through clinical trials to commercialization.

For over 35 years, Maravai's brands have served as proven catalysts for innovative, lifesaving technology for humanity. Across our portfolio of brands, we hold numerous patents and offer more than 1,500 innovative products and services, including CleanCap® technology, CleanAmp® hot start PCR, Antibody Affinity Extraction™, EndonucleaseGTP®, PROTEIN A MIX-N-GO™, MockV®, Sterling™, Glen Pak™, Glen Gel-Pak™.

Each of our brands are ISO-9001:2015 certified and have earned hundreds of thousands of citations in peer-reviewed scientific publications.
