fields.funding_data: Total funding: 606200000; Latest funding: Other (100000000) on 2025-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: 4D Molecular Therapeutics (4DMT) is a public, clinical-stage biotechnology company based in Emeryville, California. Founded in 2013, the company specializes in developing targeted gene therapies using its proprietary Therapeutic Vector Evolution (TVE) platform. This innovative platform creates customized adeno-associated virus (AAV) vectors designed to treat large-market diseases in ophthalmology and pulmonology through non-invasive delivery methods.

4DMT is advancing multiple clinical-stage gene therapy candidates, including treatments for wet age-related macular degeneration, cystic fibrosis, and geographic atrophy. The company focuses on providing durable, disease-targeted therapeutics that aim to transform treatment paradigms and reduce the burden on patients. With strategic partnerships with major biopharmaceutical companies, 4DMT is committed to progressing its product pipeline and enhancing the potential of genetic medicines for patients with genetic diseases.

raw_data.Short Description:
4D Molecular Therapeutics (4DMT) is a public, clinical-stage biotechnology company based in Emeryville, California. Founded in 2013, the company specializes in developing targeted gene therapies using its proprietary Therapeutic Vector Evolution (TVE) platform. This innovative platform creates customized adeno-associated virus (AAV) vectors designed to treat large-market diseases in ophthalmology and pulmonology through non-invasive delivery methods.

4DMT is advancing multiple clinical-stage gene therapy candidates, including treatments for wet age-related macular degeneration, cystic fibrosis, and geographic atrophy. The company focuses on providing durable, disease-targeted therapeutics that aim to transform treatment paradigms and reduce the burden on patients. With strategic partnerships with major biopharmaceutical companies, 4DMT is committed to progressing its product pipeline and enhancing the potential of genetic medicines for patients with genetic diseases.

fields.name: Precigenome LLC
fields.address: 2176 Ringwood Ave, San Jose, California, United States, 95131
fields.notes:
Description: PreciGenome LLC is a biotechnology and life sciences instrumentation company based in San Jose, California. Founded in 2017, it specializes in microfluidic technology, lipid nanoparticle (LNP) synthesis systems, and PCR instrumentation for healthcare, diagnostics, and research sectors. The company operates as a B2B entity, providing advanced analytical instruments and custom formulation services to clients globally.

PreciGenome offers a range of services, including LNP formulation, microfluidic platform development, PCR assay and instrumentation services, and Organ-on-Chip validation. Its product portfolio features scalable nanoparticle synthesis systems, high-performance PCR instruments, and integrated microfluidic platforms. The company is recognized for its innovation in microfluidic technology and its commitment to supporting scientists and healthcare professionals in drug discovery and clinical manufacturing. With a dedicated team, PreciGenome continues to drive advancements in the biotechnology field.

fields.name: GeneTex
fields.notes:
Description: GeneTex, Inc. is a biotechnology company based in Irvine, California, specializing in the research, development, and validation of high-quality antibody reagents and biomedical products for the life sciences sector. Founded in 1997, GeneTex has grown into a multinational organization with a global distribution network, including facilities in Taiwan and offices in Germany.

The company offers a wide range of services to support scientific research, including custom antibody development, rigorous product validation, and technical support. GeneTex's extensive product catalog features over 50,000 items, including primary and secondary antibodies, recombinant proteins, research kits, and specialized reagents for various research areas such as cancer biology, immunology, and neuroscience. GeneTex serves a diverse clientele, including academic researchers, clinical institutions, pharmaceutical companies, and government agencies, providing essential tools for biomedical research and product discovery.

fields.name: Atheln
fields.address: 6051 Scripps Street, San Diego, California, United States, 92122
fields.notes:
Description: Atheln helps clients create and execute integrated pharma and biotech product development and commercialization strategies. 
Our approach is unique in its integration of commercial and technical disciplines to maximize product value, relentless focus on identifying the key disciplines addressing client needs, exclusively utilizing industry seasoned experts, and being solely driven by client success. This approach is rooted in our extensive hands-on experience spanning from early research to life cycle management.

Our global team of over 100 highly specialized, industry-seasoned experts excel at risk identification and mitigation, while delivering phase appropriate and customized solutions that increase value and likelihood of success.
Each of our experts located in the US and EU have  20 - 40 years of global strategic and operational experience in Fortune 500 and start-up companies. 
Specialized teams are created for each project to address the specific needs of the client for single and/or multi-competency projects, interim, long-term or project based needs.

In thirteen years, Atheln has built up a top notch consulting practice and worked with over 120 companies in the US, Canada, Europe and Asia.


Type of Products

Biologics (recombinant, fusion and transgenic proteins, MAbs, vaccines, biosimilars, ADCs, cell and gene therapies), small molecules, combination products, medical devices.

Therapeutic Areas
Addiction, aesthetics, autoimmune disease, dermatology, endocrinology, gastroenterology, hematology, infectious diseases, immunology, nephrology, neurodegenerative diseases,  neurology, oncology, ophthalmology, pain, psychiatry, rare diseases, transplantation, and urology

fields.name: Certis Oncology Solutions
fields.address: 5626 Oberlin Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-1705
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 20000000; Latest funding: Series C (10000000) on 2024-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: Certis Oncology Solutions, Inc. is a life science technology company based in San Diego, California, founded in 2016 by James H. Berglund. The company focuses on precision oncology and translational science, aiming to connect preclinical studies with clinical trials. Certis utilizes AI-directed drug development and advanced functional testing to provide predictive therapeutic response data, assisting oncologists and pharmaceutical developers in making informed decisions about cancer treatment.

Certis offers a range of services, including Patient-Derived Orthotopic Xenograft (PDX) development, advanced imaging and histology, and personalized functional testing through its YourPDX® service. The company also provides translational science consulting to bridge the gap between preclinical models and clinical outcomes. Certis has developed proprietary products like Certis Oncology Intelligence® and CertisAI™, which enhance the analysis of tumor behavior and drug efficacy. With a CLIA-certified laboratory and a commitment to advancing cancer care, Certis is dedicated to delivering high-confidence data for both pharmaceutical companies and individual cancer patients.

Pacific BioLabs' mission is to contribute to the development of safe and efficacious medical products while maintaining rigorous scientific integrity and the highest standards of regulatory quality. We have a strong commitment to confidentiality, impartiality, and continuous quality improvement.

As The Service Leader in Life Science Testing, Pacific BioLabs continuously strives to meet and exceed client deadlines and offers personalized, proactive, and friendly client service.

Pacific BioLabs is the leading West Coast provider of GMP/GLP laboratory testing services supporting Medical Device, Pharmaceutical, and Biotechnology industries. As a CRO, we offer toxicology, biocompatibility, microbiology, sterility assurance, and analytical chemistry testing and research support services.

fields.name: ReechPharma
fields.address: 8024 Central Ave, Newark, California, United States, 94560-3450
fields.notes:
Description: ReechPharma is a specialty pharmaceutical formulation development company based in Newark, California, founded in 2018. The company focuses on creating customized formulations for biopharma companies, enhancing drug solubility, permeability, bioavailability, stability, scalability, and safety. Led by Founder & CEO Ravi Mahalingam, Ph.D., ReechPharma combines over 60 years of team expertise in various fields, including physicochemical and biopharmaceutical principles.

The company offers formulation and analytical development services, supporting drug discovery and development from early stages through lifecycle management. ReechPharma's facility is equipped with advanced technology for pharmaceutical drug development, including clean rooms and analytical labs. The firm has collaborated with over 170 pharma and biotech companies on more than 1,500 preclinical and clinical projects, providing flexible support as an extended team. ReechPharma is ISO 9001 certified and emphasizes its core values of care, creativity, and delivery.

fields.name: Abio
fields.address: 370 Convention Way, Redwood City, California, United States, 94063
fields.notes:
Description: Abio Technologies is a cutting-edge life sciences company redefining the way scientists innovate. We design AI-first, automation-native solutions that serve as the backbone for global challenges in health, sustainability, and biotechnology.


Smarter Lab. Better Data. Bolder Science. 

While laboratory hardware has advanced at breakneck speeds, R&D data management has often failed to keep pace. This digital disconnect creates silos between wet and dry labs, hindering the scalability of modern discovery. Abio bridges this gap by providing a Unified R&D Data Hub necessary to eliminate "data drudgery" and accelerate the pace of discovery.


Our Four Core Pillars 

Autonomous Lab Workflows: Transforming operations with end-to-end automation and vendor-agnostic instrument integration.

Augmented Intelligence: Unleashing data potential with built-in AI models to provide predictive insights and guide experimental design.

Precision Science: Empowering research with specialized tools for diverse modalities, from material management to experimental record keeping.

Trusted Data Fabric: Establishing reliability and traceability across the research lifecycle while ensuring compliance with FAIR principles.


Explore the future of R&D at www.abio.ai.

fields.address: 2445 Augustine Drive, Santa Clara, California, United States, 95054
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 5800000; Latest funding: Seed (5800000) on 2026-02-01
fields.notes:
Description: Expert Intelligence (EI) is an AI-driven solutions company based in Santa Clara, California, established in 2022. The company specializes in laboratory automation and data analysis for regulated sectors such as pharmaceuticals, biotech, food, and environmental testing. EI combines advanced machine learning technologies with domain expertise to enhance research capabilities and streamline workflows.

The flagship product, EI CoPilot, is a generative AI automation assistant that facilitates raw data analysis and results interpretation in laboratory settings. It automates manual analysis and quality control processes across various applications, including pharmaceutical sample analysis and food testing. EI OnSite, announced in January 2025, is an on-premises AI infrastructure solution that scales analytical lab data analysis using advanced hardware for real-time processing. EI CoPilot integrates seamlessly with laboratory instruments and Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), offering features like local data processing and automated workflow generation while ensuring compliance and security in sensitive environments.

fields.name: Immunology Consultants Laboratory, Inc.
fields.address: 15862 SW 72nd Ave, Portland, Oregon, United States, 97224-8050
fields.notes:
Description: ICL Inc., also known as Immunology Consultants Laboratory, Inc., is a U.S.-based manufacturer specializing in high-quality antibodies, ELISA kits, immunoassays, and proteins for life science research. With over 45 years of experience, the company operates from its laboratory in Portland, Oregon. Founded by Dr. Gerrie A. Leslie, ICL initially started as a consulting firm focused on custom antibody development.

The company offers a wide range of products, including primary and secondary antibodies, high-quality ELISA kits for immunoassays, host cell protein detection assays, and protein reference standards for both human and animal models. ICL also provides custom services for antibody development and characterization. Its diverse customer base includes in vitro diagnostics manufacturers, pharmaceutical and biopharma companies, leading research institutions, and individual researchers. Recently, ICL has expanded its global reach through a partnership with Morebio Inc. to enhance the distribution of its life science products in Korea.

fields.name: Aladdin Scientific

fields.notes:
Description: Aladdin Scientific Corporation is a global supplier of high-quality research chemicals, biochemicals, life science reagents, and related tools. Established in 2009 and headquartered in Riverside, California, the company employs over 700 people and operates offices in the US, Germany, Ireland, Singapore, and China. Aladdin emphasizes product quality and precision, supported by three manufacturing facilities and two R&D centers.

The company offers a wide range of reagents, including chemicals, proteins, antibodies, and specialty reagents for research-grade applications. These products are designed for laboratory analysis and measurement technology, catering to various scientific fields such as life sciences, environmental sciences, and analytical chemistry. Aladdin also provides customized expertise to help clients address challenges in science and technology, supporting research needs worldwide.

fields.name: Defined Bioscience, Inc.
fields.address: 6404 Nancy Ridge Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-2248
fields.notes:
Description: Defined Bioscience is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, California, founded in 2020. The company focuses on developing chemically defined, animal-origin-free cell culture media, supplements, and reagents. Their mission is to enhance the growth of cells for stem cell research and cultivated meat production while minimizing animal dependency. Defined Bioscience aims to provide reproducible, cost-effective, and high-quality outcomes through their innovative media solutions.

The company offers a range of products, including the HiDef-B8 stem cell maintenance medium, which allows for weekend-free passaging, and various animal-free variants of fibroblast growth factor (FGF2). Their product line supports high-density expansion and differentiation of induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) across various research and biomanufacturing applications. Defined Bioscience serves a diverse clientele, including academic researchers, biotechnology startups, pharmaceutical companies, and the cultivated meat industry, contributing to advancements in stem cell research, regenerative medicine, and cellular agriculture.

raw_data.Short Description:
Defined Bioscience is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, California, founded in 2020. The company focuses on developing chemically defined, animal-origin-free cell culture media, supplements, and reagents. Their mission is to enhance the growth of cells for stem cell research and cultivated meat production while minimizing animal dependency. Defined Bioscience aims to provide reproducible, cost-effective, and high-quality outcomes through their innovative media solutions.

The company offers a range of products, including the HiDef-B8 stem cell maintenance medium, which allows for weekend-free passaging, and various animal-free variants of fibroblast growth factor (FGF2). Their product line supports high-density expansion and differentiation of induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) across various research and biomanufacturing applications. Defined Bioscience serves a diverse clientele, including academic researchers, biotechnology startups, pharmaceutical companies, and the cultivated meat industry, contributing to advancements in stem cell research, regenerative medicine, and cellular agriculture.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 4406700000; Latest funding: Debt Financing (4000000000) on 2020-02-01
fields.notes:
Description: Amgen Inc. is a leading biotechnology company based in Thousand Oaks, California, and one of the largest independent biopharmaceutical firms globally. Founded in 1980, Amgen has played a significant role in the biotechnology industry, being the first to commercialize recombinant DNA-based medicines. The company operates in over 100 countries and employs approximately 31,500 people.

Amgen focuses on the discovery, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of human therapeutics, specializing in biologics such as recombinant proteins and monoclonal antibodies. Its diverse portfolio includes treatments for oncology, cardiovascular disease, inflammation, bone health, nephrology, and rare diseases. Notable products include Neupogen for infection risk reduction in cancer patients, Repatha for lowering cholesterol, and Enbrel for autoimmune disorders. Amgen is also a leader in the biosimilars market, providing cost-effective alternatives to established biologic drugs. The company is committed to addressing serious health challenges and improving patient outcomes through innovative therapies.

raw_data.Short Description:
Amgen Inc. is a leading biotechnology company based in Thousand Oaks, California, and one of the largest independent biopharmaceutical firms globally. Founded in 1980, Amgen has played a significant role in the biotechnology industry, being the first to commercialize recombinant DNA-based medicines. The company operates in over 100 countries and employs approximately 31,500 people.

Amgen focuses on the discovery, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of human therapeutics, specializing in biologics such as recombinant proteins and monoclonal antibodies. Its diverse portfolio includes treatments for oncology, cardiovascular disease, inflammation, bone health, nephrology, and rare diseases. Notable products include Neupogen for infection risk reduction in cancer patients, Repatha for lowering cholesterol, and Enbrel for autoimmune disorders. Amgen is also a leader in the biosimilars market, providing cost-effective alternatives to established biologic drugs. The company is committed to addressing serious health challenges and improving patient outcomes through innovative therapies.

fields.name: OPM Biosciences
fields.address: 5653 Stoneridge Dr, Pleasanton, California, United States, 94588-8543
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 72509800; Latest funding: Series B (43509800) on 2020-08-01
fields.notes:
Description: OPM Biosciences is a biotechnology company based in Shanghai, China, with a dedicated R&D team in Pleasanton, California. Established in 2013, OPM specializes in cell culture media solutions and Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) services for biopharmaceuticals. The company employs between 201 and 500 people and is recognized as a leading provider in China's biotechnology sector.

OPM offers a wide range of catalog and customized cell culture media products, adhering to cGMP guidelines. These products support the development of therapeutic monoclonal antibodies, vaccines, cell therapies, and gene therapies. Additionally, OPM provides comprehensive CDMO services that cover the entire biologics development lifecycle, from DNA to drug application filings. The company operates advanced facilities equipped with single-use bioreactors and maintains dual GMP manufacturing sites.

With a global clientele of over 1,000 pharmaceutical and biotech companies, OPM has played a significant role in advancing more than 200 molecules currently in clinical or commercial phases. The company aims to partner with biopharmaceutical firms to optimize drug development costs and improve access to high-quality biopharmaceuticals for patients.

fields.name: Meissner
fields.address: 1001 Flynn Rd, Camarillo, California, United States, 93012
fields.notes:
Description: Meissner Corporation is a leading provider of advanced microfiltration products and single-use systems, primarily serving the pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical industries. Founded in 1984 and based in Camarillo, California, the company has established itself as one of the largest privately held entities in the bioprocessing sector. With a significant manufacturing campus in California and a facility in Ireland, Meissner supports clients globally through a network of offices and field staff.

The company offers a wide range of services, including the design and manufacturing of microfiltration systems, development of single-use fluid handling assemblies, and integrity testing and validation services. Meissner is recognized for its rapid growth and commitment to innovation, employing around 500 to 1,000 people worldwide. Its products are essential for various applications, including drug development, vaccine production, and bioprocessing, ensuring high standards of quality and compliance with regulatory requirements.

fields.name: Illumina
fields.address: 5200 Illumina Way, San Diego, California, United States, 92122
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 124000000; Latest funding: Other (96000000) on 2000-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Illumina, Inc. is a leading biotechnology company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 1998, it specializes in developing and marketing DNA sequencing and array-based technologies. The company aims to advance precision health and research discovery, serving customers in over 155 countries across various sectors, including research, clinical, and applied markets.

Illumina's product offerings include integrated sequencing systems and consumables for DNA, RNA, and protein analysis, as well as microarray solutions for genotyping and gene expression. The company also provides clinical diagnostics, such as the Galleri test for early cancer detection, and noninvasive prenatal testing. In addition to its products, Illumina offers sequencing and genotyping services, support and training, and clinical workflow development to enhance cancer diagnosis and other applications. With a workforce of around 8,970 employees, Illumina reported revenue of approximately $4.34 billion in 2025.

Keywords: medical specialities, health care, biotechnology research, health, wellness & fitness

raw_data.Short Description:
Illumina, Inc. is a leading biotechnology company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 1998, it specializes in developing and marketing DNA sequencing and array-based technologies. The company aims to advance precision health and research discovery, serving customers in over 155 countries across various sectors, including research, clinical, and applied markets.

Illumina's product offerings include integrated sequencing systems and consumables for DNA, RNA, and protein analysis, as well as microarray solutions for genotyping and gene expression. The company also provides clinical diagnostics, such as the Galleri test for early cancer detection, and noninvasive prenatal testing. In addition to its products, Illumina offers sequencing and genotyping services, support and training, and clinical workflow development to enhance cancer diagnosis and other applications. With a workforce of around 8,970 employees, Illumina reported revenue of approximately $4.34 billion in 2025.

fields.name: Credence MedSystems
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 84900000; Latest funding: Venture (Round not Specified) (39900000) on 2021-09-01
fields.notes:
Description: Credence MedSystems is an industry-recognized and award-winning innovator, addressing unmet needs in injectable drug delivery. With infrastructure and competencies to design, develop, and commercialize new products, we enable our Pharma and Biotech partners to bring new therapies to patients across the world.

Guided by our Innovation Without Change® philosophy, Credence develops solutions that enhance safety, usability, and performance while integrating seamlessly into existing workflows and manufacturing processes. Our core product platforms include the Companion® prefilled syringes, Dual Chamber delivery systems, and Metered-Dosing technologies, supporting a broad range of therapeutic applications.
 
By reducing development risk, accelerating time-to-market, and improving experiences for patients, caregivers, and healthcare providers, Credence helps our pharmaceutical and biotechnology partners bring life changing injectable therapies to market with confidence.

fields.name: American Peptide Company
fields.notes:
Description: The American Peptide Society, APS, a nonprofit scientific and educational organization founded in 1990, provides a forum for advancing and promoting knowledge of the chemistry and biology of peptides. The approximately eighteen-hundred members of the Society come from North America and from more than forty-five other countries throughout the world. The establishment of the American Peptide Society was a result of the rapid worldwide growth that has occurred in peptide-related research, and of the increasing interaction of peptide scientists with virtually all fields of science.

Peptides are chains of amino acid residues with remarkable biological functions, ranging from hormonal regulation to antibiotic activities. Members of the American Peptide Society are involved in research in academia, industry, and government, covering all aspects of peptide chemistry, biology, and pharmaceutical science. Research topics include synthesis of biologically important targets, isolation and characterization of new products, structure-activity relationship studies, molecular diversity, de novo design, drug delivery, and the discovery of new pharmaceutical agents.

Keywords: non-profit organizations, b2b, services, non-profit, nonprofit organization management

fields.name: Aroga Biosciences
fields.address: Sorrento Valley Rd, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.notes:
Description: Aroga Biosciences, Inc. is a Contract Research Organization (CRO) based in San Diego, California, founded in 2019. The company specializes in regulatory medical writing, quality control, publishing, and submission services for biotech and pharmaceutical companies. Aroga supports the development of therapeutics from early stages through regulatory approval, focusing on areas such as oncology, rare diseases, neurology, and cell and gene therapy.

Aroga offers a comprehensive range of services, including medical and regulatory writing, quality control checks, and regulatory operations for FDA submissions. The team consists of experienced PhDs, PharmDs, and scientists who ensure technical accuracy and compliance. Aroga is recognized for its commitment to scientific storytelling and fast turnaround times, having been named a Top 10 Medical Writing Service Company in 2023 and 2024 by Life Sciences Review. The company aims to reduce vendor dependency by providing end-to-end support for submission-ready documents tailored to the needs of its clients in the life sciences sector.

fields.name: Huntington Medical Research Institutes
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 1000000; Latest funding: Other (1000000) on 2020-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: HMRI (Huntington Medical Research Institutes) is an independent, nonprofit biomedical research organization based in Pasadena, California. Founded in 1952, HMRI has a rich history of scientific discovery that has led to advancements in diagnostics, medical devices, and therapies, positively impacting millions of patients globally. The institute operates from a state-of-the-art research campus and focuses on key areas such as neuroscience, cardiovascular disease, and liver and gut health.

HMRI conducts both basic and applied research, including clinical studies and trials that translate findings into practical medical solutions. The organization is also dedicated to education, inspiring future scientists through various programs. With a mission to improve health outcomes and a vision to be a leading biomedical research organization by 2032, HMRI continues to innovate and collaborate with medical institutions, government agencies, and academic partners to address complex health challenges.

Our team of expert scientists and state-of-the-art facilities allow us to produce reliable, consistent, and specific antibodies for your specific needs. Founded in 2012, Qoolabs has been committed to excellence with focus on delivering the best possible antibodies to our customers. We take pride in our culture of innovation and collaboration. With our core technology QMAb quick monoclonal antibody discovery platform, our mission is to provide high-quality antibody solutions empower our clients to reach their full potential and achieve their goals. Our QMAb platform is designed with simplicity in mind, making it highly customizable to meet the unique needs of each of our clients. Whether you're searching for antibodies to a small molecule or a large complex, Qoolabs has a solution that can help you to advance your research. If you're looking for a partner that can help you achieve your goals, contact us to discuss more details.

fields.name: Tioga Research
fields.address: 6330 Nancy Ridge Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.notes:
Description: Tioga Research is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, CA, founded in 2011. It specializes in research and early development services for skin-applied formulations, including topical drugs, transdermal products, cosmetics, and personal care items. As a contract research organization (CRO), Tioga integrates proprietary high-throughput experimentation platforms and formulation development strategies to enhance the drug development process for its clients.

The company offers a range of services, including formulation screening and development, regulatory support for various pathways, and testing and analytics. Tioga is recognized for its innovative tools like INSIGHT™, STORM™, and TORNADO™, which facilitate efficient evaluation of formulation variables. Additionally, Tioga provides seamless integration from research to clinical and commercial manufacturing through its parent company, Encube Ethicals. This allows for a smooth transition from concept to market for pharmaceutical and cosmetic companies.

fields.name: Zymo Research
fields.address: 17062 Murphy Avenue, Irvine, California, United States, 92614
fields.notes:
Description: Zymo Research Corp is a privately held biotechnology company based in Irvine, California, specializing in the development and manufacturing of molecular biology research tools. Founded in 1994 by Larry Jia, the company has grown from a small garage operation into a global leader in the field, serving academic institutions, government laboratories, and biotech firms worldwide.

Zymo Research offers a wide range of products and services, including DNA and RNA purification kits, epigenetics tools, microbiome research kits, and next-generation sequencing (NGS) solutions. The company emphasizes high quality, reliability, and affordability in its offerings, which are designed to simplify complex scientific workflows. With a strong focus on innovation, Zymo Research supports researchers with technical guidance and custom solutions tailored to their experimental needs. The company employs approximately 250 to 280 people and has established a robust international distribution network to ensure rapid delivery of its products.

fields.address: 101 E Kennedy Blvd, Tampa, Florida, United States, 33602
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 17720000000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (16500000000) on 2024-02-01
fields.notes:
Description: Catalent, Inc. is a prominent global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Tampa, Florida, with a corporate office in Somerset, New Jersey. The company operates over 50 facilities across four continents, focusing on the pharmaceutical, biotech, and consumer health industries. Catalent employs approximately 16,900 people and supports more than 7,000 products for over 1,000 customers worldwide.

Catalent offers comprehensive development solutions and advanced delivery technologies for drugs, biologics, and consumer health products. Its core capabilities include biologics manufacturing, softgel and oral technologies, oral and specialty delivery, and clinical supply services. Key offerings encompass pre-formulation and formulation, analytical testing, manufacturing of various dosage forms, clinical supply and packaging, and regulatory consulting. The company aims to enable life science innovators to successfully develop and launch products that prioritize patient health.

raw_data.Short Description:
Catalent, Inc. is a prominent global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Tampa, Florida, with a corporate office in Somerset, New Jersey. The company operates over 50 facilities across four continents, focusing on the pharmaceutical, biotech, and consumer health industries. Catalent employs approximately 16,900 people and supports more than 7,000 products for over 1,000 customers worldwide.

Catalent offers comprehensive development solutions and advanced delivery technologies for drugs, biologics, and consumer health products. Its core capabilities include biologics manufacturing, softgel and oral technologies, oral and specialty delivery, and clinical supply services. Key offerings encompass pre-formulation and formulation, analytical testing, manufacturing of various dosage forms, clinical supply and packaging, and regulatory consulting. The company aims to enable life science innovators to successfully develop and launch products that prioritize patient health.

fields.notes:
Description: Our Vision:
"Every drug can be made and every disease can be treated" by building an open-access platform with the most comprehensive capabilities and technologies in the global biologics industry.

Our Mission:
To accelerate and transform the discovery, development and manufacturing of biologics through a comprehensive open-access platform, enabling our global healthcare partners and benefiting patients worldwide.

Our Company:
WuXi Biologics (stock code: 2269.HK), a Hong Kong-listed company, is a leading global open-access biologics technology platform offering end-to-end solutions to empower organizations to discover, develop, and manufacture biologics from concept to commercial manufacturing. The company's history and achievements demonstrate its commitment to providing a truly ONE-stop service offering and strong value proposition to its global clients. With total estimated capacity for biopharmaceutical production planned in the U.S., China, Ireland, Germany, and Singapore exceeding 430,000 liters after 2024, WuXi Biologics will provide its biomanufacturing partners with a robust and premier-quality global supply chain network.

fields.name: Ascensus Specialties
fields.address: 2821 Northup Way, Bellevue, Washington, United States, 98004
fields.product_category: Borohydrides, organometallics, process-development and GMP/API-support chemistry
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2021-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Ascensus Specialties is a prominent U.S.-based chemical manufacturer specializing in high-quality specialty materials. Founded in 1957 and headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, the company operates globally with production facilities in Elma, WA, Evans City, PA, Newburyport, MA, and Cambridge, UK. With a team of approximately 200–500 employees and a strong technical foundation supported by over 20 PhDs, Ascensus is the only Western-based provider of its core chemistries.

The company offers a diverse range of products, including sodium borohydride, boranes, reactive and alkali metals, CVD/ALD precursors, and high-purity active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Ascensus also provides custom synthesis and specialized reagents for various applications in pharmaceuticals, microelectronics, petrochemicals, and more. Additionally, the company operates the Strem Catalog, supplying high-purity research-grade materials to support innovation in laboratories. Ascensus Specialties is committed to enhancing client processes and expanding its global reach while maintaining high manufacturing standards.

Ascensus also provides custom synthesis and specialized reagents for various applications in pharmaceuticals, microelectronics, petrochemicals, and more. Additionally, the company operates the Strem Catalog, supplying high-purity research-grade materials to support innovation in laboratories. Ascensus Specialties is committed to enhancing client processes and expanding its global reach while maintaining high manufacturing standards.
raw_data.Product Category: Borohydrides, organometallics, process-development and GMP/API-support chemistry

fields.name: Jubilant HollisterStier

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 17315976; Latest funding: Debt Financing (17315976) on 2023-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Jubilant HollisterStier CMO is a prominent pharmaceutical contract manufacturer based in Spokane, Washington, with a significant facility in Montreal, Quebec. The company specializes in producing sterile injectable, ophthalmic, lyophilized, and topical pharmaceutical products. As a subsidiary of Jubilant Pharmova Limited, it has a rich history dating back to 1921 and is recognized for its commitment to manufacturing excellence and regulatory compliance.

The company offers a range of services, including sterile fill-finish, lyophilization, ophthalmic manufacturing, and clinical trial manufacturing. It supports pharmaceutical developers and biopharmaceutical brands by providing reliable, large-scale commercialization of life-saving drugs. With ongoing expansions at its Spokane facility, Jubilant HollisterStier continues to enhance its capabilities, ensuring it meets the evolving needs of global healthcare markets.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 162551960; Latest funding: Other (45207200) on 2025-05-01
fields.notes:
Description: PolyPeptide Group AG is a leading Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) that specializes in the development and manufacturing of peptide-based and oligonucleotide-based active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Established in 1952 in Malmö, Sweden, and incorporated in 1996, the company has over 70 years of experience in therapeutic peptide production. Headquartered in Baar, Switzerland, PolyPeptide operates six GMP-certified facilities across Europe, the United States, and India.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services throughout the drug development lifecycle, including custom peptide synthesis, oligonucleotide manufacturing, API development, and regulatory support. PolyPeptide is recognized for manufacturing a significant portion of the world's approved peptide drug substances, supporting pharmaceutical and biotech companies in launching innovative therapies for various diseases. Its diverse product portfolio includes high-quality GMP-grade peptides, oligonucleotides, and personalized medicine components, catering to a global customer base that includes pharmaceutical companies, biotech firms, and academic institutions.

fields.name: Takara Bio USA
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 95600000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (50000000) on 2016-05-01
fields.notes:
Description: Takara Bio USA, Inc. is a subsidiary of Takara Bio Inc., a leader in biotechnology research and development based in Japan. The company operates as the primary North American hub for research, marketing, quality assurance, and eCommerce. In 2021, Takara Bio USA moved to a new 129,000-square-foot facility in San Jose, California, which supports its expansion and advanced research initiatives. Additional facilities are located in Madison, Wisconsin, and Ann Arbor, Michigan.

The company offers a wide range of services to support life sciences researchers, including contract development and manufacturing, custom research solutions, technical consultation, and logistics for global distribution. Takara Bio USA manufactures and distributes research-use-only kits, reagents, and instruments across various fields such as genomics, cell biology, and protein science. Their product brands—Takara®, Clontech®, and Cellartis®—focus on different aspects of life sciences research, providing essential tools for academic, industrial, and biopharmaceutical clients.

raw_data.Short Description:
Takara Bio USA, Inc. is a subsidiary of Takara Bio Inc., a leader in biotechnology research and development based in Japan. The company operates as the primary North American hub for research, marketing, quality assurance, and eCommerce. In 2021, Takara Bio USA moved to a new 129,000-square-foot facility in San Jose, California, which supports its expansion and advanced research initiatives. Additional facilities are located in Madison, Wisconsin, and Ann Arbor, Michigan.

The company offers a wide range of services to support life sciences researchers, including contract development and manufacturing, custom research solutions, technical consultation, and logistics for global distribution. Takara Bio USA manufactures and distributes research-use-only kits, reagents, and instruments across various fields such as genomics, cell biology, and protein science. Their product brands—Takara®, Clontech®, and Cellartis®—focus on different aspects of life sciences research, providing essential tools for academic, industrial, and biopharmaceutical clients.

fields.name: Silgan Containers
fields.address: 21600 Oxnard Street, Los Angeles, California, United States, 91367
fields.notes:
Description: Silgan Containers LLC is the largest provider of metal food packaging solutions in the United States and a leading manufacturer in North America. Established in 1987, the company has roots in can-making that date back to 1899. Headquartered in Woodland Hills, California, Silgan Containers operates over 20 production facilities across the U.S. and has a dedicated Technical Center near Milwaukee, Wisconsin. As a subsidiary of Silgan Holdings Inc., the company plays a significant role in a global portfolio with annual net sales of approximately $5.9 billion.

Silgan Containers specializes in metal food packaging, offering a range of products including steel and aluminum food containers, custom-designed containers, and specialized packaging for pet food. The company is committed to sustainability, focusing on increasing recycled content, modernizing manufacturing processes, and utilizing renewable energy. Silgan Containers partners with major brands in the food and consumer goods sectors, providing tailored manufacturing solutions and technical expertise to meet specific customer needs.

fields.name: Accelagen
fields.notes:
Description: Accelagen Pty Ltd is a global Contract Research Organisation (CRO) based in Hawthorn East, Melbourne. The company specializes in end-to-end clinical trial development and regulatory affairs for the biopharmaceutical and medical device industries. Founded in 2010, Accelagen has rapidly grown into a multi-million dollar business, leveraging over 150 years of combined experience in drug and device development.

Accelagen offers a comprehensive range of services that cover the entire product lifecycle, including clinical trial management, regulatory strategy planning, biometrics and safety management, and medical writing. The company supports clients from small biotech firms to large multinational pharmaceutical companies, providing tailored solutions to navigate complex regulatory environments. With a focus on co-creating meaningful outcomes, Accelagen aims to accelerate products to market and enhance human health through strategic partnerships and integrated development solutions.

fields.name: AvantGen
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Other on 2021-05-01
fields.notes:
Description: AVANTGEN is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, founded in 2006. The company specializes in the discovery, engineering, and optimization of antibodies for therapeutic and diagnostic applications. It serves various sectors, including pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, diagnostics, and government, through services, partnerships, and licensing.

AVANTGEN operates two main divisions: the Therapeutic Division focuses on human antibody discovery and optimization, while the Diagnostics/Research Division develops rabbit monoclonal antibodies for diagnostic targets. The company utilizes a proprietary yeast display system and fully human antibody libraries, which include over 100 billion antibody clones, to facilitate rapid antibody generation and screening. AVANTGEN also offers services such as antibody humanization, affinity maturation, and antibody engineering.

Among its products, AVANTGEN has developed the AccuRate™ COVID-19 SelfCheck OTC Test and provides rabbit monoclonal antibodies for research. The company collaborates with various partners, including pharmaceutical and biotechnology firms, government entities, and academic institutions, to accelerate the development of antibody-based therapeutics.

fields.name: Biopharma Technology
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Other on 2018-03-01
fields.notes:
Description: Biopharma Group has dedicated divisions covering the UK, France, Ireland, and USA. We meet the needs of our customers'​ projects appropriate to the size and stage by augmenting their in-house expertise whether to buy equipment, a single cycle run/ analysis or a full formulation development programme. 

Biopharma Group is a leading provider of comprehensive solutions for the pharma, diagnostics, and biotech industries. We offer a wide range of equipment, services, and expertise to support your R&D and manufacturing needs to streamline your processes, and production to accelerate time-to-market.

Our equipment offerings (to UK, Ireland & France) range from freeze dryers, solvent evaporation systems & aseptic fill-finish lines to bioreactors, TFF systems, cryopreservation storage solutions, & clean airflow lab equipment. 

Biopharma Group uniquely also offers specialist CDMO services for customers internationally who are producing liquid formulations and/or lyophilized products as part of their product processing. These services range from feasibility studies, R&D formulation & cycle development, process reviews and lyo lab analysis through to contract manufacturing (GMP & non-GMP), consultancy and lyo training courses, with the aim to get your products safely and efficiently to market, fast.

Biopharma Group is renowned for its industry-leading expertise and its unwavering commitment to quality. We are a trusted partner to leading companies across the pharma, diagnostics, and biotech sectors.

Whether you're a pharmaceutical giant or a growing biotech startup, Biopharma Group has the solutions and support you need to succeed.

raw_data.Short Description:
Biopharma Group has dedicated divisions covering the UK, France, Ireland, and USA. We meet the needs of our customers'​ projects appropriate to the size and stage by augmenting their in-house expertise whether to buy equipment, a single cycle run/ analysis or a full formulation development programme. 

Biopharma Group is a leading provider of comprehensive solutions for the pharma, diagnostics, and biotech industries. We offer a wide range of equipment, services, and expertise to support your R&D and manufacturing needs to streamline your processes, and production to accelerate time-to-market.

Our equipment offerings (to UK, Ireland & France) range from freeze dryers, solvent evaporation systems & aseptic fill-finish lines to bioreactors, TFF systems, cryopreservation storage solutions, & clean airflow lab equipment. 

Biopharma Group uniquely also offers specialist CDMO services for customers internationally who are producing liquid formulations and/or lyophilized products as part of their product processing. These services range from feasibility studies, R&D formulation & cycle development, process reviews and lyo lab analysis through to contract manufacturing (GMP & non-GMP), consultancy and lyo training courses, with the aim to get your products safely and efficiently to market, fast.

Biopharma Group is renowned for its industry-leading expertise and its unwavering commitment to quality. We are a trusted partner to leading companies across the pharma, diagnostics, and biotech sectors.

Whether you're a pharmaceutical giant or a growing biotech startup, Biopharma Group has the solutions and support you need to succeed.

fields.name: ImpriMed
fields.address: 3980 Fabian Way, Palo Alto, California, United States, 94303-4605
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 40850000; Latest funding: Other on 2023-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: ImpriMed, Inc. is a Silicon Valley-based company specializing in AI-driven precision medicine for oncology. Founded in 2017 by Dr. Sungwon Lim and others, it focuses on developing personalized cancer treatment solutions through live-cell drug sensitivity assays and advanced machine learning. Initially targeting veterinary oncology for dogs and cats, ImpriMed has expanded its services to include human oncology and pharmaceutical clinical research organization (CRO) services.

The company’s proprietary platform utilizes ex vivo live-cell drug sensitivity testing combined with machine learning to predict individual responses to cancer therapies. ImpriMed offers a range of services for veterinary and human oncologists, as well as pharmaceutical companies, helping to optimize treatment decisions and improve patient outcomes. With a mission to empower medical professionals, ImpriMed aims to enhance cancer care through data-driven therapy selection.

fields.name: R2M Pharma
fields.address: 600 Gateway Blvd, South San Francisco, California, United States, 94080-7014
fields.notes:
Description: R2M Pharma, Inc. is a collaborative pharmaceutical research organization based in South San Francisco, California. Founded in 2017, the company specializes in accelerating drug discovery and development for biotech firms, particularly those that may not have in-house medicinal chemistry expertise. R2M Pharma operates as a privately-held professional services firm with a dedicated team of experienced scientists.

The company offers a wide range of services, including structural biology, compound synthesis, target validation, lead optimization, and drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics (DMPK) analysis. R2M Pharma also provides patent strategy consultation and personalized project management. With state-of-the-art analytical instrumentation and a network of strategic partners, R2M Pharma supports clients throughout the drug discovery and development process. The leadership team, headed by founder Julio Medina, Ph.D., brings extensive experience in drug discovery across various therapeutic areas.

fields.name: ACRO Biotech
fields.notes:
Description: Acro Biotech, Inc. is a California-based manufacturer specializing in in-vitro diagnostic (IVD) rapid tests. Founded in 2005 and headquartered in Rancho Cucamonga, the company focuses on high-volume, non-invasive diagnostic products for both the US and international markets. Acro Biotech is committed to providing cost-effective, rapid, on-site medical diagnosis through innovative and quality products.

The company develops and manufactures a range of lateral flow rapid tests and associated analyzers for various clinical applications, including drugs of abuse, women’s health, cardiac and tumor markers, infectious diseases, and veterinary diagnostics. Their products are designed to work with multiple specimen types, such as urine, blood, and oral fluid. Acro Biotech also offers diagnostic hardware, including test readers and analyzers. With a team of experienced professionals, the company operates primarily in the B2B space, serving healthcare providers, clinics, and diagnostic laboratories globally.

fields.name: 2T Labs LLC
fields.address: 5752 Oberlin Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-1747
fields.notes:
Description: 2T Labs is a scientist-led Contract Research Organization (CRO) specializing in custom ELISA and PCR-based assay development for the biotech and pharmaceutical industries.

We combine academic rigor with industry efficiency, delivering validated, regulatory-compliant in vitro testing solutions that meet the highest standards of quality, speed, and affordability.

Our expertise includes:

Assay development and validation (ELISA, PCR, HPLC)

Cell-based functional and viability assays

Biomarker quantification and statistical analysis

ADA and immunogenicity testing

With transparent pricing, fast turnaround, and personalized service, we help clients move from discovery to data-driven decision-making — faster.

Biomarker quantification and statistical analysis

ADA and immunogenicity testing

fields.notes:
Description: HiComp Microtech is an original design manufacturer based in Suzhou, China, with a sales and innovation center in Mountain View, USA. Founded in 2014, the company specializes in high-quality precision components and products utilizing micro and nanotechnologies. HiComp operates a 100,000 square foot ISO 13485 certified foundry, equipped with cleanrooms and a GMP facility, capable of producing 50 million units annually.

The company offers design-to-manufacturing services, including precision injection molding, bonding and assembly, and process development. HiComp serves various industries such as life sciences, MEMS, advanced packaging, microfluidics, and biomedical consumables. They focus on creating components from polymer, glass, ceramic, and metal substrates, catering to both established companies and startups. Dr. Jing Chen, the CEO and founder, leads the company with extensive experience in microfluidics and manufacturing engineering.

fields.name: Confidence Pharmaceutical Research
fields.address: 702 Marshall Street, Redwood City, California, United States, 94063
fields.notes:
Description: Confidence Pharmaceutical Research is a global full-service Contract Research Organization (CRO) with over 30 years of experience in clinical trial management. Based in Burlingame, California, the company operates in more than 35 countries and is led by medical doctors, ensuring a strong foundation in medical expertise for its clinical research operations.

The company offers a wide range of services, including clinical monitoring, data management, regulatory submissions, medical writing, project management, and patient recruitment strategies. Confidence has been involved in over 350 clinical studies across more than 25 therapeutic areas, such as oncology, neurology, and cardiovascular disorders. Their team consists of experienced professionals, with Clinical Research Associates averaging 16 years of experience and Project Managers averaging 23 years in the field. 

Confidence Pharmaceutical Research is known for its efficient trial design and management, achieving notable milestones like rapid patient enrollment and site startup. The company emphasizes customized solutions tailored to the specific needs of its sponsors.

fields.name: ProMedix
fields.address: 4640 S Macadam Ave, Portland, Oregon, United States, 97239-4232
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 860000; Latest funding: Other (200000) on 2024-12-01
fields.notes:
Description: ProMedix Inc is a medical device startup based in Newport Beach, California, specializing in precision patient monitoring solutions. The company develops noninvasive, fingertip-based monitors aimed at enhancing the recognition and management of circulatory dysfunction. Their mission is to transform how circulatory health is monitored and managed.

The flagship product, the PeriFRL Monitor, is designed for the rapid detection of peripheral blood flow issues. It employs PeriFRL CRT (Capillary Refill Time) technology to provide standardized and accurate monitoring results. This innovative approach allows healthcare providers to identify circulatory dysfunction early, improving patient outcomes. ProMedix's technology is applicable in various settings, including emergency care, critical care, and home monitoring for circulatory conditions.

Description: TechForce Robotics is a Robotics-as-a-Service Provider delivering fully managed autonomous service robots that support operational teams. The industries we can serve include hospitality, healthcare, education, venues, airports, and other service environments.

Our robots handle physically demanding, repetitive transport work like moving linens, waste, and supplies, as well as beverage service automation in high demand settings. Through a subscription-based model, we manage deployment, mapping, monitoring, and ongoing support so organizations can adopt automation without taking on complex technology.

The focus is simple. Support the workforce. Strengthen daily operations. Deliver automation that works in real world environments.

fields.name: VivoTex
fields.address: Newberg, Oregon, United States, 97132
fields.notes:
Description: VivoTex offers 3D printed microfiber scaffolds designed to help researchers replicate tissue models more effectively than other scaffolds, matrices, or bioinks. Our proprietary melt electrowriting (MEW) technology—a cutting-edge 3D printing technique—creates highly precise, customizable scaffolds at the human cellular scale. This innovative process provides enhanced control over the cell micro-environment, accelerating cell growth, improving cell-to-cell communication, and boosting viability.  Ideal for in vitro tissue engineering, cancer research, and drug discovery, customers may choose from a catalog of application-specific scaffolds or work with our engineering team to create a custom-designed solution that meets their study needs.

fields.address: 8999 Biolegend Way, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 5250000000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (5250000000) on 2021-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: BioLegend is a global leader in developing and manufacturing high-quality antibodies, recombinant proteins, and research reagents for biomedical and life science research. Founded in June 2002 in San Diego, California, the company aims to accelerate scientific discovery by providing researchers with valuable tools. In September 2021, BioLegend was acquired by PerkinElmer and now operates as a distinct brand under Revvity.

The company offers a wide range of services, including custom conjugation, assay development, sample testing, and technical support. BioLegend's extensive product portfolio includes over 17,000 products, focusing on immunology, neuroscience, cancer research, and cell biology. Their offerings feature fluorophore-conjugated antibodies, recombinant proteins, flow cytometry reagents, and ELISA kits, among others. BioLegend serves a diverse clientele, including academic institutions, biotechnology companies, diagnostic laboratories, and hospitals, supporting various research applications.

raw_data.Short Description:
BioLegend is a global leader in developing and manufacturing high-quality antibodies, recombinant proteins, and research reagents for biomedical and life science research. Founded in June 2002 in San Diego, California, the company aims to accelerate scientific discovery by providing researchers with valuable tools. In September 2021, BioLegend was acquired by PerkinElmer and now operates as a distinct brand under Revvity.

The company offers a wide range of services, including custom conjugation, assay development, sample testing, and technical support. BioLegend's extensive product portfolio includes over 17,000 products, focusing on immunology, neuroscience, cancer research, and cell biology. Their offerings feature fluorophore-conjugated antibodies, recombinant proteins, flow cytometry reagents, and ELISA kits, among others. BioLegend serves a diverse clientele, including academic institutions, biotechnology companies, diagnostic laboratories, and hospitals, supporting various research applications.

fields.name: Accelsiors CRO
fields.address: 2 Bahnhof-Park, Baar, Canton of Zug, Switzerland, 6340
fields.notes:
Description: Accelsiors CRO is a full-service, global Contract Research Organization founded in 2002 and based in Baar, Switzerland. The company supports the biopharmaceutical industry throughout all phases of clinical drug development, offering rapid and cost-effective clinical trial services across various therapeutic areas. With 27 offices in over 40 countries, Accelsiors combines mid-sized agility with a broad global reach.

The company provides end-to-end clinical research solutions, including study design, regulatory guidance, data management, and pharmacovigilance services. Accelsiors specializes in complex disease indications such as biosimilars, orphan indications, and rare diseases, and serves a diverse clientele ranging from emerging biotechnology startups to multinational pharmaceutical companies. With over 20 years of experience, Accelsiors is dedicated to accelerating therapies for complex diseases through science-driven, patient-centric approaches.

fields.name: Eurofins CDMO Alphora
fields.address: Mississauga, Ontario, Canada
fields.notes:
Description: Eurofins CDMO Alphora Inc. is a prominent global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Mississauga, Ontario, Canada. Established in 2003, the company specializes in the development and production of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs), drug products (DPs), and biologics. It operates an FDA-inspected and Health Canada-approved facility, employing over 250 people. The company is enhancing its capabilities with a new biologics manufacturing facility, supported by a significant investment from Canada's Strategic Innovation Fund.

The company offers a wide range of services, including small molecule development, drug product formulation, and biologics manufacturing. Eurofins CDMO Alphora is known for its integrated approach, providing end-to-end solutions from pre-clinical discovery to commercial manufacturing. Its clientele includes pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies, as well as academic institutions, all seeking specialized support for drug development and manufacturing. With a focus on complex small molecules and biologic therapeutics, Eurofins CDMO Alphora plays a vital role in the global life sciences industry.

fields.name: Jumpcode Genomics
fields.address: 4755 Nexus Center Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-3051
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 36148326; Latest funding: Other (3458827) on 2024-08-01
fields.notes:
Description: Jumpcode Genomics is a biotechnology company founded in 2016 and based in San Diego, California. The company specializes in enhancing next-generation sequencing (NGS) data using its proprietary CRISPRclean® technology. This innovative approach removes unwanted nucleic acid sequences, improving sensitivity and reducing bias in genomic analyses.

Jumpcode Genomics offers a variety of kits designed for different applications, including the CRISPRclean RNA Prep kits, DepleteX® Human DNA Depletion Kit, and Single Cell RNA Boost Kit. These products support single-cell analysis, microbiome studies, whole transcriptome profiling, and liquid biopsy applications. The company serves researchers in academic and research institutions globally, focusing on fields such as oncology, infectious diseases, and consumer genomics. Their mission is to empower scientists by breaking technical barriers in genomics, enabling the detection of previously hidden signals.

fields.notes:
Description: Abterra Bio is enabling the next-generation of antibody therapeutics.  Our technologies use the latest in next-generation sequencing, mass spectrometry, and machine learning methods to comprehensively map the antibody response to challenge.  We give our partners access to every relevant antibody to meet their project goals. Find out what service might be right for you with our service guide: https://abterrabio.com/service-guide/

Abterra Bio began life as Digital Proteomics LLC, a spin-out of the University of California – San Diego. Founders Pavel Pevzner and Vineet Bafna are professors in the Computer Science and Engineering Department and leading researchers in bioinformatics. Their expertise forms the algorithmic foundation upon which our products and services are built.

fields.name: Oncodesign Services
fields.address: 20 Rue Jean Mazen, Dijon, Bourgogne-Franche-Comte, France, 21000
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 24200000; Latest funding: Venture (Round not Specified) (24200000) on 2024-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: Oncodesign Services supports biopharma and biotechnology teams navigating critical preclinical decisions as programs progress toward the clinic. At this stage, generating data alone is not enough; what matters is whether the evidence genuinely supports the next step forward.

We are a preclinical contract research organization specializing in advanced development programs in targeted radiotherapy, oncology, immuno-oncology, and inflammation and autoimmune diseases. Our work is focused on the translational phase of drug development, where scientific rigor, careful study design, and clear interpretation are essential to support confident progression toward IND and clinical development.

In targeted radiotherapy, we support programs from early candidate through IND-enabling studies, addressing challenges including radionuclide supply, biodistribution imaging, dosing strategy, and translational relevance.

Our preclinical pharmacology work spans oncology, immuno-oncology, and immune-mediated inflammatory diseases, with studies designed around disease biology, proof of concept, and mechanism of action. Imaging approaches are integrated where appropriate to support longitudinal assessment of pharmacological effects, biodistribution, and target engagement. Model selection and development are tailored to the biological and translational questions driving each study.

Our experience includes over 30 years in preclinical oncology, more than 20 years in imaging, and over 10 years in inflammatory disease and radiopharmaceutical development. Our facilities are AAALAC-accredited, reflecting internationally recognized standards for the humane and responsible use of animals in research.

fields.address: 5020 Brandin Ct, Fremont, California, United States, 94538-3140
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 8000000; Latest funding: Series A (8000000) on 2017-09-01
fields.notes:
Description: Paragon Genomics is a precision medicine company based in the San Francisco Bay Area, founded in 2015. The company specializes in advanced target enrichment and library preparation solutions for Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS). Paragon Genomics focuses on enhancing throughput, efficiency, and accuracy in targeted sequencing workflows. With approximately $8 million in funding, the company employs a dedicated team of scientists and engineers committed to advancing genomics research.

The company offers a range of services, including ultra-high multiplex PCR-based target enrichment technologies, high-performance library preparation kits, and custom NGS panels tailored to specific research needs. Paragon Genomics is known for its CleanPlex® technology platform, which improves the accuracy and sensitivity of targeted sequencing. Their products support various applications, such as precision oncology, infectious disease testing, and inherited disease screening, making them valuable to research laboratories, clinical diagnostic labs, and biopharmaceutical companies.

fields.name: Bora Biologics and Bora Pharmaceuticals
fields.address: 10394 Pacific Center Ct, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-4340
fields.notes:
Description: Bora Biologics is a technology-driven Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Zhubei, Taiwan, with additional facilities in San Diego, California. As a division of Bora Pharmaceuticals, the company specializes in the development and manufacturing of mammalian and microbial biologics. With 14 years of experience, Bora Biologics has successfully developed 45 biologics and biosimilars and delivered 100 cGMP batches.

The company offers a wide range of integrated services throughout the biologics lifecycle, including discovery research support, cell line and strain development, protein preparation and purification, process development, and both early-phase and commercial manufacturing. Bora Biologics emphasizes scalable processes, regulatory compliance, and quality, serving biotech and pharmaceutical clients globally. With a team of 125 experts, the company is committed to delivering tailored solutions that reduce timelines, costs, and risks in bringing complex biologics to market.

fields.name: DC3 Therapeutics
fields.address: 163 W Harris Ave, South San Francisco, California, United States, 94080-6008
fields.notes:
Description: DC3 Therapeutics, LLC is a Contract Research Organization (CRO) based in South San Francisco, California. Founded in 2013 by experienced scientists, the company specializes in providing comprehensive support for drug discovery, development, and translation. With a focus on customer service and efficiency, DC3 Therapeutics offers a range of services, including pre-clinical and IND-enabling studies, biomarker assays, and integrated lab services. 

The company operates a CLIA-accredited lab and emphasizes collaboration with clients to design experiments that meet their specific research goals. DC3 Therapeutics serves biotech, pharmaceutical companies, and research institutions, acting as a responsive partner throughout the drug development process. With a team of fewer than 25 employees, the company leverages over 50 years of combined expertise to deliver high-quality results in a timely manner.

fields.name: HTD Biosystems
fields.address: 3197 Independence Drive, Livermore, California, United States, 94551
fields.notes:
Description: HTD Biosystems is a contract research organization (CRO) based in Livermore, California, founded in 2001. The company specializes in accelerating the development of biopharmaceuticals, biologics, vaccines, and advanced drug delivery systems. With a small team of 2-10 employees, HTD emphasizes flexibility, innovation, and collaboration, leveraging its expertise in Design of Experiments (DoE 2.0) and high-throughput technologies to deliver robust results.

HTD offers comprehensive formulation and process development services for a range of biological candidates, including parenteral drugs, biosimilars, and proteins. Their key specialties include nanoparticle characterization and formulation development, protein formulation, vaccine development, and drug delivery systems. The company has collaborated with various organizations, supporting both biotech startups and large pharmaceutical firms in drug discovery and clinical development.

fields.name: Broadley-James
fields.address: 19 Thomas, Irvine, California, United States, 92618-2704
fields.notes:
Description: Broadley-James Corporation is a manufacturer specializing in pH, dissolved oxygen (DO), and redox sensors, as well as bioreactors and automation systems. Founded in 1967 and based in Irvine, California, the company has over five decades of experience in sensor technology, primarily serving bioprocessing, life science, and industrial applications, including the food and beverage sector. With a workforce of 51-200 employees, Broadley-James generates approximately $24.1 million in annual revenue.

The company offers a wide range of products, including single-use sensors designed for easy integration into bioprocessing, conventional sensors with advanced features, and specialized industrial sensors for demanding environments. Broadley-James emphasizes stability and low-drift performance in its sensor designs and invests significantly in research and development to maintain a strong intellectual property portfolio. Its solutions cater to biotechnology research, manufacturing, and product design, supporting users from laboratory settings to large-scale production.

The company offers a wide range of products, including single-use sensors designed for easy integration into bioprocessing, conventional sensors with advanced features, and specialized industrial sensors for demanding environments. Broadley-James emphasizes stability and low-drift performance in its sensor designs and invests significantly in research and development to maintain a strong intellectual property portfolio. Its solutions cater to biotechnology research, manufacturing, and product design, supporting users from laboratory settings to large-scale production.

fields.name: Crown Bioscience San Diego
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 265665998; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (204000000) on 2025-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: Crown Bioscience is a global contract research organization (CRO) based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2006 and now a division of JSR Life Sciences, the company specializes in preclinical and translational research services aimed at accelerating drug discovery and development, particularly in oncology and immuno-oncology. With over 1,300 experts, including PhD and MS scientists, Crown Bioscience operates a network of research centers across the United States, Europe, and the Asia-Pacific region.

The company offers a wide range of integrated solutions throughout the drug development timeline. Their services include preclinical research, translational platforms that connect preclinical findings to clinical outcomes, and clinical trial support. Crown Bioscience is known for its specialized oncology services, including the world's largest Patient-Derived Xenograft (PDX) collection and exclusive tumor organoid services using HUB technology. They also provide advanced laboratory services, biological models, reagents, and access to extensive oncology databases, making them a valuable partner for pharmaceutical and biotechnology firms.

fields.name: HD Biosciences
fields.address: Shanghai, Shanghai, China
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition on 2017-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: HD Biosciences Co., Ltd is a preclinical drug discovery contract research organization (CRO) based in Pudong, Shanghai, China. Founded in 2004, it became a subsidiary of WuXi AppTec in May 2017. The company operates facilities in Shanghai, San Diego, California, and Cranbury, New Jersey, employing between 201-500 people and serving the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries globally.

HD Biosciences offers a range of drug discovery and development services, including target validation, pharmacology and screening, hit identification, therapeutic antibody discovery, and in vivo pharmacology. The company is recognized as a leader in preclinical drug R&D services, collaborating with eight of the world's ten largest pharmaceutical companies and forming strategic partnerships to enhance clients' portfolio development. Its strong scientific expertise and commitment to quality have established HD Biosciences as a trusted name in the industry.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 23550000; Latest funding: Venture (Round not Specified) (5000000) on 2024-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Meadowhawk Biolabs, Inc. is a privately held Contract Research Organization (CRO) that specializes in preclinical services for the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industries. Founded in 2021, the company is headquartered in Marlborough, Massachusetts, with a secondary facility in Hayward, California. Meadowhawk aims to accelerate drug discovery and development by providing rapid and reliable laboratory services.

The company offers a range of bioanalytical services, including LCMS bioanalysis, immunoanalytical services, rodent in-vivo studies, assay development, and pharmacology and pharmacokinetics. Their advanced technology platforms enable quantitative analysis of novel chemical entities, therapeutic proteins, and biologics. Meadowhawk's bicoastal presence allows for efficient collaboration and quick turnaround times, making it a strategic partner for pharmaceutical and biotechnology firms focused on early-stage drug development.

fields.name: Pharmaron Lab Services
fields.address: Irvine, California, United States
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 457500000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (137500000) on 2020-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: Pharmaron, officially known as Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd., is a leading global Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRO/CDMO) based in Beijing, China. Established in 2004, the company provides comprehensive drug discovery and development solutions, supporting clients from early-stage synthetic chemistry to preclinical safety assessment, clinical trials, and commercial-scale manufacturing. Pharmaron operates across China, the United States, and the United Kingdom, employing around 21,000 staff and serving over 2,200 partners, including all of the top 20 global pharmaceutical companies.

The company offers a wide range of services organized into three main segments: Laboratory Services, Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) Services, and Clinical Development Services. Pharmaron specializes in small molecules, biologics, cell and gene therapies, and antibody-drug conjugates, with capabilities for both drug substance and drug product production at clinical and commercial scales. Its integrated business model ensures seamless collaboration across regions, enabling clients to access a unified platform for drug development and manufacturing.
