raw_data.Short Description:
Pharmaron, officially known as Pharmaron Beijing Co., Ltd., is a leading global Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRO/CDMO) based in Beijing, China. Established in 2004, the company provides comprehensive drug discovery and development solutions, supporting clients from early-stage synthetic chemistry to preclinical safety assessment, clinical trials, and commercial-scale manufacturing. Pharmaron operates across China, the United States, and the United Kingdom, employing around 21,000 staff and serving over 2,200 partners, including all of the top 20 global pharmaceutical companies.

The company offers a wide range of services organized into three main segments: Laboratory Services, Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls (CMC) Services, and Clinical Development Services. Pharmaron specializes in small molecules, biologics, cell and gene therapies, and antibody-drug conjugates, with capabilities for both drug substance and drug product production at clinical and commercial scales. Its integrated business model ensures seamless collaboration across regions, enabling clients to access a unified platform for drug development and manufacturing.

fields.name: SciQuus Oncology
fields.address: 4225 Executive Sq, San Diego, California, United States, 92037
fields.notes:
Description: SciQuus Oncology is a dedicated Contract Research Organization (CRO) focused on advancing cancer research through high-quality clinical trial services. Founded in 2013 and based in San Diego, California, the company specializes in oncology and hematologic drug development, particularly First-in-Human (FiH) studies for novel cancer therapies. With a team of over 20 professionals, SciQuus Oncology combines extensive experience across the drug development lifecycle, from pre-IND planning to managing large registration trials.

The company offers a comprehensive range of services, including drug development planning, clinical trial management, and regulatory support. They work closely with biopharmaceutical sponsors and biotech firms, providing tailored solutions to navigate the complexities of oncology research. SciQuus Oncology is committed to improving the lives of cancer patients by delivering efficient and innovative clinical trial services while maintaining high standards of quality and integrity.

fields.name: Antheia
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 262330000; Latest funding: Series C (24000000) on 2026-01-01
fields.notes:
Description: Antheia, Inc. is a biomanufacturing company focused on enhancing pharmaceutical supply chains through advanced biosynthesis and synthetic biology. Founded in 2015 by Dr. Christina Smolke and Dr. Kristy Hawkins, the company programs yeast cells to produce complex pharmaceutical ingredients at an industrial scale. Headquartered in Menlo Park, California, Antheia aims to end drug shortages by utilizing sustainable fermentation processes instead of traditional agricultural and petrochemical inputs.

The company offers a range of services, including industrial-scale fermentation for Key Starting Materials (KSMs) and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs), as well as whole-cell engineering to create plant-inspired medicines. Antheia's platform supports rapid-response manufacturing, ensuring reliable access to essential medicines for global pharmaceutical companies and healthcare organizations. With a focus on sustainability and resilience, Antheia is committed to addressing the challenges of modern healthcare and improving access to critical medicines.

fields.name: Hygia Reagents Inc
fields.address: 9530 Padgett St, San Diego, California, United States, 92126-4448
fields.notes:
Description: Hygia Reagents Inc. is a biotechnology research and manufacturing company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2022, the company specializes in high-quality reagents, custom formulations, and small-scale production for various bioscience applications. Hygia Reagents operates ISO 9001-certified, GMP-compliant laboratory facilities, featuring over 2,000 square feet of Grade 1000 cleanrooms that support sterile manufacturing for medical devices, pharmaceuticals, and semiconductor research.

The company manufactures precast PAGE gels and cell culture media, along with customizable reagents, buffers, and ultra-pure water. Hygia Reagents also offers IVD diagnostics, CMO/CDMO services, and analytical testing services. Their recently launched 0.5 mL Form-Fill-Seal single-dose liquid filling line enhances their product offerings. Serving clients in biotechnology research, healthcare, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and semiconductor manufacturing, Hygia Reagents is positioned as a valuable partner for both local startups and larger pharmaceutical firms.

Keywords: manufacturing, b2b, services, mechanical or industrial engineering

fields.name: Arcturus Therapeutics
fields.address: 10628 Science Center Drive, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 310376096; Latest funding: Other (115000000) on 2023-08-01
fields.notes:
Description: Arcturus Therapeutics Holdings Inc. is a commercial messenger RNA (mRNA) medicines company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2013, the company focuses on developing therapeutics for rare liver and respiratory diseases, as well as vaccines for infectious diseases. Arcturus specializes in the discovery, development, and commercialization of mRNA medicines aimed at addressing unmet medical needs, particularly in rare genetic disorders and pandemic threats.

The company utilizes three core technologies: LUNAR®, a proprietary lipid-mediated delivery system; STARR®, a self-amplifying mRNA technology platform; and its manufacturing expertise in mRNA drug production. Arcturus has developed KOSTAIVE®, the first approved self-amplifying mRNA COVID-19 vaccine, and has a robust pipeline of drug candidates in various stages of development, including treatments for cystic fibrosis and OTC deficiency. Additionally, Arcturus engages in strategic collaborations and provides manufacturing services through its joint venture in Japan, enhancing its capabilities in mRNA vaccine production.

Arcturus Therapeutics Holdings Inc. is a commercial messenger RNA (mRNA) medicines company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2013, the company focuses on developing therapeutics for rare liver and respiratory diseases, as well as vaccines for infectious diseases. Arcturus specializes in the discovery, development, and commercialization of mRNA medicines aimed at addressing unmet medical needs, particularly in rare genetic disorders and pandemic threats.

The company utilizes three core technologies: LUNAR®, a proprietary lipid-mediated delivery system; STARR®, a self-amplifying mRNA technology platform; and its manufacturing expertise in mRNA drug production. Arcturus has developed KOSTAIVE®, the first approved self-amplifying mRNA COVID-19 vaccine, and has a robust pipeline of drug candidates in various stages of development, including treatments for cystic fibrosis and OTC deficiency. Additionally, Arcturus engages in strategic collaborations and provides manufacturing services through its joint venture in Japan, enhancing its capabilities in mRNA vaccine production.

fields.name: Clutch Biotechnologies
fields.address: San Francisco, California, United States
fields.notes:
Description: DNA cloning is a common pain point in biological research. These extremely custom workflows often consume significant amounts of researcher time, lack transferability, and inhibit rapid experimental iteration.

At ClutchBio, we eliminate time you spend cloning so you can focus on the science. Our full suite of DNA design and cloning services includes specialized software, plasmid cloning services, and a biorepository. Together, these will improve your team's productivity by removing cloning as the typical bottleneck for starting experiments and research projects.

fields.funding_data: Total funding: 1400000000; Latest funding: Other on 2026-05-01
fields.notes:
Description: Bio-Rad Laboratories, Inc. is a leading global manufacturer and distributor of life science research products, clinical diagnostics, and analytical instrumentation. Founded in 1952 in Berkeley, California, the company is headquartered in Hercules, California, and employs approximately 8,000 to 8,300 people. Bio-Rad serves over 70,000 customers in more than 150 countries, supported by a network of over 30 subsidiary offices worldwide.

The company operates through two main segments: Life Science and Clinical Diagnostics. The Life Science segment provides researchers with tools for separating, purifying, and characterizing biological materials, including reagents and laboratory instruments. The Clinical Diagnostics segment focuses on designing and manufacturing diagnostic solutions for medical laboratories, offering test systems and specialized test kits for a wide range of clinical applications. Bio-Rad's extensive product portfolio includes laboratory instruments, reagents, software solutions, and specialized kits, catering to academic institutions, pharmaceutical companies, clinical laboratories, and more.

fields.name: Rapid Novor
fields.address: 137 Glasgow St, Kitchener, Ontario, Canada, N2G 4X8
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 5000000; Latest funding: Series A (5000000) on 2020-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Rapid Novor Inc. is the world's leader in antibody protein sequencing technology. Specializing in the field of mass spectrometry-based proteomics, the team has developed the technology to directly sequence antibody proteins without needing to access the producing cell line. As a University of Waterloo spin-off, the company is building its technology portfolios based on the twenty years of scientific research and inventions from Dr. Bin Ma, co-founder and chief scientist. The company's REmAb™ antibody protein sequencing service has allowed for the accurate sequencing of any given antibody proteins on a routine basis. The company's WILD™ service is the first commercially available service that can accurately distinguish the isobaric Isoleucine and Leucine using mass spectrometry. 

The company's mission is to advance life science for better human health with next generation protein sequencing.

For more information, visit www.rapidnovor.com. Follow the company on Twitter @rapidnovor.

fields.name: Ajinomoto Bio-Pharma Services
fields.address: Chuo, Tokyo, Japan
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (0) on 2025-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: Ajinomoto Bio-Pharma Services, also known as Aji Bio-Pharma, is a global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) with over 40 years of experience. The company specializes in process development, cGMP manufacturing, and drug product fill-finish services for the biotechnology and pharmaceutical sectors. As part of the Ajinomoto Group, it utilizes advanced bioscience and fine chemical technologies to manufacture medicines for critical diseases, including cancer and genetic disorders.

With manufacturing and development sites in Belgium, the United States, Japan, and India, Ajinomoto Bio-Pharma Services offers a wide range of services throughout the drug development lifecycle. These include molecule-specific manufacturing for small and large molecules, oligonucleotides, and gene therapies, as well as process development and optimization. The company is committed to quality and innovation, providing tailored solutions to a diverse client base that includes biotechnology startups and major pharmaceutical corporations.

Description: Bionova Scientific is a full-service biologics Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) that specializes in developing and manufacturing recombinant protein therapeutics and monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) using mammalian cell culture processes. Founded in 2014 and acquired by Asahi Kasei Bioprocess America in 2022, the company is headquartered in Fremont, California, with additional facilities in the San Francisco Bay Area and a dedicated plasmid DNA facility in Texas.

Bionova offers comprehensive services throughout the drug development lifecycle, including process development, pilot and GMP manufacturing, and formulation development. The company has recently expanded its capabilities to include plasmid DNA (pDNA) process development and manufacturing. Bionova primarily serves biologic drug development companies and innovators in the biopharmaceutical industry, helping them accelerate their therapies to market. With a focus on quality and flexibility, Bionova is committed to supporting clients from early-stage discovery to commercial launch.

fields.name: REV Clinical
fields.address: 6501 Truxtun Ave, Bakersfield, California, United States, 93309-0633
fields.notes:
Description: CBCC Global Research is a full-service contract research organization (CRO) based in Bakersfield, California, with an office in Ahmedabad, India, and remote teams in Japan, China, and Eastern Europe. With over 30 years of experience, the company specializes in clinical research for pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and diagnostics, operating across more than 400 trial sites.

The company focuses on therapeutic areas such as oncology, ophthalmology, neuro/psychiatry, and dermatology. CBCC Global Research offers a wide range of clinical research services for Phase I-IV trials, including trial monitoring, data management, safety management, and regulatory consulting. It is closely associated with the Comprehensive Blood & Cancer Center, leveraging its expertise for clinical trials and partnerships, notably with the UCLA Jonsson Comprehensive Cancer Center TRIO-US network for early-access trials.

fields.notes:
Description: PBS Biotech aims to be the leading provider of innovative, single-use bioreactor systems specifically designed to address the unique challenges of cultivating shear sensitive cell types for cell therapy development.  Our offering includes scalable, cost-effective disposable solutions designed to take you from benchtop R&D to large-scale cGMP manufacturing, and expert process development services to help solve cell culture challenges and accelerate your path to commercialization.
 
To learn more, contact our sales team at sales@pbsbiotech.com or visit our website at www.pbsbiotech.com

fields.name: BPS Bioscience
fields.address: 6405 Mira Mesa Blvd, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-4119
fields.notes:
Description: BPS Bioscience Inc. is a biotechnology company based in San Diego, California, founded in 2005. The company specializes in developing and manufacturing high-quality research tools for drug discovery, including recombinant enzymes, cell lines, assay kits, and lentiviruses. Their focus areas include immunotherapy, epigenetics, CRISPR, and cell signaling.

With a team of Ph.D. experts, BPS Bioscience offers over 4,500 products that support drug discovery and biomedical research. They also operate as a boutique Contract Research Organization (CRO), providing custom services such as protein expression, assay development, cell line engineering, and compound screening. Their mission is to advance scientific discoveries that lead to therapies, ensuring continuous quality improvement through ISO 9001:2015 certification. BPS Bioscience serves academic researchers and pharmaceutical companies, helping to accelerate biological discoveries and the development of treatments for human diseases.

fields.name: ACON Laboratories
fields.address: 9440 Carroll Park Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-5201
fields.notes:
Description: ACON Laboratories is an international diagnostic and medical device company based in San Diego, California. Founded in 1996, the company has over 25 years of experience in developing and marketing affordable, high-quality medical diagnostic products. ACON operates in more than 130 countries and is known for its commitment to making healthcare accessible worldwide.

The company offers a wide range of In-Vitro Diagnostic (IVD) products, including blood glucose monitoring systems, urinalysis strips, rapid tests for various infections, and molecular diagnostic tests. ACON serves healthcare professionals, institutions, and direct consumers, providing solutions for point-of-care testing. Additionally, it supplies animal health products for veterinary services. ACON Laboratories maintains a manufacturing facility in Hangzhou, China, which is FDA registered and ISO 13485:2016 certified, ensuring high standards in production.

fields.name: ChemDiv
fields.address: 6605 Nancy Ridge Dr, San Diego, California, United States, 92121
fields.notes:
Description: ChemDiv, Inc., also known as Chemical Diversity, is a leading Contract Research Organization (CRO) based in San Diego, California. Founded in 1990 by Alexander Ivachtchenko, the company provides comprehensive research and development services to pharmaceutical, biotechnology, and academic institutions. With over 30 years of experience, ChemDiv offers end-to-end solutions from target identification to clinical proof of concept and market access.

The company operates globally, with research and development subsidiaries in Russia, Ukraine, China, Germany, and Portugal. ChemDiv specializes in drug discovery, preclinical development, and clinical support, utilizing advanced technologies such as AI/ML and high-throughput screening. It boasts the world's largest small molecule inventory, with over 1.5 million compounds, and offers a unique e-commerce platform for easy access to its resources. ChemDiv partners with top pharmaceutical and biotech companies, facilitating the development of innovative therapies across various therapeutic areas.

fields.name: MabPlex USA
fields.address: 5965 Village Way, San Diego, California, United States, 92130-2427
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 130103500; Latest funding: Series B (71000000) on 2020-06-01
fields.notes:
Description: MabPlex USA, Inc. is a Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in San Diego, California. Founded in 2015, it is part of MabPlex International, which has a global presence in biologics development and manufacturing. The company operates with a team of 11-50 employees and maintains R&D and manufacturing facilities in both the United States and China, totaling over 60,000 square meters of space.

MabPlex offers comprehensive end-to-end CDMO services that cover the entire biopharmaceutical development and manufacturing lifecycle. Their capabilities include contract development services, manufacturing from DNA to finished drug products, and specialized expertise in antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). The company is recognized for its work with monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, and bispecific antibodies. With a strong track record in regulatory submissions and clinical development, MabPlex has successfully completed numerous submissions and IND filings, collaborating with biopharmaceutical companies worldwide.

fields.name: Neuroservice USA
fields.address: Le Puy-Sainte-Reparade, Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur, France, 13610
fields.notes:
Description: Neuroservices-Alliance is a specialized Contract Research Organization (CRO) focused on drug discovery for the Central Nervous System (CNS) and Pain sectors. Founded in 2018 and based in Le Puy-Sainte-Réparade, France, the company serves the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries globally. It operates as a consortium of expert neuroscientists, providing high-quality, translational data to support research programs.

The company offers a range of services, including translational electrophysiology and behavioral testing, with both in vitro and in vivo solutions. Their capabilities include advanced techniques such as Multi-Electrode Array (MEA), Patch-Clamp, and optogenetics, utilizing various tissue models from rodents to human-derived neurons. Neuroservices-Alliance emphasizes customized protocols and direct communication with clients to meet specific research goals, ensuring flexibility and responsiveness in their operations.

fields.name: Onepot AI
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 13000000; Latest funding: Seed (13000000) on 2025-11-01
fields.notes:
Description: Onepot.AI is a San Francisco-based startup founded in March 2024 by Daniil Boiko and Andrei Tyrin. The company specializes in AI-driven automation of chemical synthesis, aiming to accelerate small molecule discovery in drug development and materials science. Onepot.AI emerged from stealth in November 2025, securing $13 million in funding to develop automated labs that address synthesis bottlenecks.

The company's main product, POT-1, is a fully automated synthesis lab powered by Phil, an AI organic chemist. Users can submit target molecules or choose from a digital library of 3.4 billion compounds. Phil generates synthesis plans, selects reagents, and directs robots for execution and analysis. Onepot.AI's technology delivers compounds 6-10 times faster than traditional methods, with median lead times under 10 business days. The platform supports various reactions and focuses on enhancing the Design-Make-Test-Analyze cycles in biotech, pharma, and materials science.

Keywords: pharmaceutical manufacturing, b2b, saas, services, pharmaceuticals, medical, computer software, information technology & services

Technologies: Gmail, Google Apps, Amazon AWS

fields.name: Puracyp
fields.address: 5900 Sea Lion Pl, Carlsbad, California, United States, 92010
fields.notes:
Description: We are a biotechnology company dedicated to the development of methods that permit rapid and efficient assessment of the safety of new drugs prior to their clinical usage. Specifically, we offer cell-based systems to examine nuclear receptor activation, the first step in drug-metabolizing enzyme induction. The primary nuclear receptor assays are those important in drug discovery and include human and rat PXR (SXR) and AhR. Our other services include studies to determine the metabolic stabilily, kinetics of metabolism, P450 reaction phenotyping, and inhibition of P450 oxidation by new chemical entities. We have devised systems that are high in throughput without sacrificing quality. Puracyp also offers tools to study oxidative drug metabolism in vitro, including human liver microsomes that have been extensively characterized for P450 enzyme expression and specific antibodies to the individual P450s.

Keywords: induction of drug metabolizing enzymes, nuclear receptor activation, pxr & ahr, human & animal receptors, biotechnology research

Technologies: Outlook, Microsoft Office 365, Apache, Google Font API, Mobile Friendly, Circle, Remote

fields.name: Sai Life Sciences
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 347172420; Latest funding: Other (173502420) on 2025-06-01
fields.notes:
Description: Sai Life Sciences Ltd is a leading Contract Research, Development, and Manufacturing Organization (CRDMO) based in India, specializing in small molecule new chemical entities (NCEs). Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Hyderabad, the company offers comprehensive services that cover the entire drug development value chain, from early drug discovery to commercial manufacturing. It operates FDA-approved facilities in Bidar and Hyderabad, and has a global presence with offices in the USA and the UK.

The company partners with innovator pharmaceutical and biotechnology firms, providing solutions for complex small-molecule drug development. Sai Life Sciences is recognized for its scientific rigor and operational excellence, aiming to support its partners in launching 25 new medicines by 2025. As the only India-based CRDMO of scale with both CRO and CDMO capabilities, it plays a vital role in the global pharmaceutical landscape.

fields.notes:
Description: SanaClis is a global Clinical Research Organization (CRO) based in Bratislava, Slovakia, founded in 2000 by experienced professionals from the pharmaceutical and CRO industries. The company specializes in integrated clinical supply chain solutions, offering comprehensive services for clinical trials throughout their lifecycle.

With a strong presence in Europe and Central and Eastern Europe, including Ukraine, Georgia, and Russia, SanaClis provides a wide range of services. These include clinical monitoring, management of investigational medicinal products and clinical trial supplies, warehousing and distribution, customs clearance, and regulatory support. The company serves sponsors of clinical trials, including pharmaceutical and biotech firms, adapting its service model to meet the specific needs of its clients. raw_data.Short Description:
SanaClis is a global Clinical Research Organization (CRO) based in Bratislava, Slovakia, founded in 2000 by experienced professionals from the pharmaceutical and CRO industries. The company specializes in integrated clinical supply chain solutions, offering comprehensive services for clinical trials throughout their lifecycle.

With a strong presence in Europe and Central and Eastern Europe, including Ukraine, Georgia, and Russia, SanaClis provides a wide range of services. These include clinical monitoring, management of investigational medicinal products and clinical trial supplies, warehousing and distribution, customs clearance, and regulatory support. The company serves sponsors of clinical trials, including pharmaceutical and biotech firms, adapting its service model to meet the specific needs of its clients. SanaClis employs approximately 218 people and generates annual revenue of around $17.4 million.

fields.name: Sapient
fields.address: 10421 Wateridge Cir, San Diego, California, United States, 92121-5781
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 13800000; Latest funding: Other (9200000) on 2022-07-01
fields.notes:
Description: Sapient is a biomarker discovery organization focused on mapping circulating chemistries in humans through large-scale small molecule biomarker profiling. Based in San Diego, California, the company aims to enhance precision drug development by generating comprehensive molecular data across various omics layers, including proteomics, metabolomics, and lipidomics. Their proprietary Human Biology Database, which includes data from hundreds of thousands of biosamples, supports the rapid identification and validation of biomarkers related to health, disease, and drug responsiveness.

The company offers a range of multi-omics services characterized by high-throughput capabilities and quick turnaround times. Their flagship technology, the rapid liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (rLC-MS) platform, allows for the analysis of over 11,000 small molecule factors per biosample. Sapient also provides targeted panel development and population-scale discovery services, catering to biopharma sponsors, research institutions, and global health organizations. Their work is recognized for its contributions to precision medicine and global health initiatives.

fields.name: Stason Pharmaceutical
fields.address: 11 Morgan, Irvine, California, United States, 92618
fields.notes:
Description: STASON PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. is a privately held Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in the United States, with over 30 years of experience in the generic drug industry. Founded in 1994, the company operates from its headquarters in Irvine, California, and an additional location in Coral Springs, Florida. STASON specializes in high-containment and potent compounds, providing a range of services from formulation development to commercial manufacturing.

The company offers comprehensive consulting and contract services tailored to drug development companies. Its capabilities include formulation and analytical development, clinical trial materials manufacturing, and contract packaging. STASON focuses on oncology, renal, and orphan indications, and has expanded into consumer supplement manufacturing. With a commitment to innovation and adherence to industry standards, STASON delivers end-to-end solutions for pharmaceutical development and commercialization.

fields.name: Therapeutics
fields.funding_data: Total funding: 1433000000; Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition (795000000) on 2025-06-01
fields.notes:
Description: Sage Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company based in Cambridge, Massachusetts. Founded in 2010, the company focuses on developing innovative brain health medicines for central nervous system (CNS) disorders, with a primary emphasis on postpartum depression (PPD). Sage Therapeutics operates globally, with subsidiaries in the US, Bermuda, Switzerland, and the UK, and is dedicated to exploring new scientific pathways to improve treatment for brain disorders.

The company's flagship product, Zurzuvae (zuranolone), is an FDA-approved treatment for postpartum depression in adults. Sage Therapeutics also has a diverse pipeline of candidates targeting various CNS conditions, including seizures and neurodevelopmental disorders. Notable pipeline products include SAGE-324, SAGE-319, and Dalzanemdor (SAGE-718), among others. The company collaborates with major biopharmaceutical partners, including Biogen and Shionogi, and is now part of Supernus Pharmaceuticals following its acquisition in 2025. Sage Therapeutics is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the ticker SAGE.

raw_data.Short Description:
The Swiss-based Bachem Group is a public, innovation-driven company specializing in the development and manufacturing of peptides and oligonucleotides. 

With over 50 years of experience and expertise Bachem provides products for research, clinical development and commercial application to pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies worldwide and offers a comprehensive range of services.

Bachem operates internationally with headquarters in Switzerland and locations in Europe, the US and Asia.
raw_data.Product Category: Peptide and oligonucleotide GMP API development/manufacturing

fields.name: Agno Pharma (Eugene)
fields.notes:
Description: Agno Pharma is a global Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO) based in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania. With over 20 years of experience, the company specializes in pharmaceutical innovation, offering a wide range of services from preclinical research to commercial production. Founded in 2004, Agno Pharma has evolved from an analytical CRO to a leader in active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) development, drug product formulation, and cGMP manufacturing.

The company operates facilities in the USA and China, combining North American pharmaceutical technologies with Chinese manufacturing capabilities. Agno Pharma provides integrated services, including the development and manufacturing of small-molecule APIs, high-potency APIs, sterile formulations, and complex drug products. They also offer analytical and regulatory services to ensure compliance with global standards. Agno Pharma serves a diverse clientele, including major pharmaceutical companies, biotech firms, and generic manufacturers, known for their expertise in complex chemistry and formulation needs.

fields.name: Calbiotech, Inc.
fields.address: 1935 Cordell Ct, El Cajon, California, United States, 92020
fields.funding_data: Latest funding: Merger / Acquisition on 2017-04-01
fields.notes:
Description: Calbiotech, Inc. is a leading U.S. company specializing in immunoassay development and manufacturing. Founded in 1998 and based in El Cajon, California, the company focuses on creating in vitro diagnostics (IVDs) for both human and animal health research. Calbiotech operates under stringent quality standards, including cGMP and ISO 13485, in an FDA-registered facility.

The company offers a wide range of services, including contract development and manufacturing for immunoassay and molecular testing programs. They provide automation services for high-throughput workflows and custom design of immunoassay products. Calbiotech's product portfolio includes over 200 items, such as ELISA kits and CLIA assays for various diseases, as well as development reagents and antibody production to support research needs. They serve a diverse clientele in the life sciences sector, including major laboratories, pharmaceutical companies, and academic institutions worldwide.

fields.name: Automated Engineering Services (AES)
fields.notes:
Description: AES (Automated Engineering Services) is a full end-to-end partner for biotech and biopharma manufacturing.

We don't just integrate systems—we design, build, automate, manufacture, validate, and even staff entire operations from lab scale to full commercial production.

Unlike traditional integrators, AES is 100% focused on life sciences. We live this industry every day, which means we understand the science, the process, the regulations, and the urgency required to bring therapies to market.

A true end-to-end model. We support the entire facility and process lifecycle:

• Process & facility design
• Custom or configurable equipment manufacturing (OEM)
• Automation, integration & data connectivity
• Commissioning, qualification & validation (CQV)
• Staffing, consulting, and operational support

Equipment built for your process—not the other way around

We manufacture modular, configurable, and fully custom equipment across upstream, downstream, and cross-process operations. Whether it's bioreactors, perfusion, liquid handling, filtration, TFF, chromatography, automation platforms—or something that has never existed before—we engineer solutions to fit your bioprocess, not generic standards.

We support the full spectrum of biotech and biomanufacturing, including:

• Cell Therapy
• Gene Therapy
• Antibodies & Recombinant Proteins
• Vaccine Production
• CMO / CDMO Operations
• Cultured Foods / Alternative Proteins
• Industrial Biotechnology

Global reach. Fast deployment. Real execution.

Our worldwide footprint and mobility infrastructure allow us to mobilize teams quickly—with permits, visas, and site access fast-tracked—so projects don't stall.

Why AES?
Because we build more than equipment.
We build the ecosystem that makes manufacturing work—efficiently, compliantly, and at scale.

AES is where engineering, equipment, and innovation converge to bring bioprocesses to life
